column1: Android | FALCON | Onboarding | Display Falcon Images based on connected Model and Cushion installed (PLACEHOLDER)
column34:
As a user, I want the onboarding experience to display images that correspond to the Falcon headset connected to my mobile device so that the instructions I see accurately reflect the device I am setting up.
h2. Acceptance Criteria (currently bring discussed - this may still change) (!)
 - Dynamically Display Falcon Images based on connected Model and Cushion installed on the frames
 - When the device is not connected, fal

!screenshot-1.png|width=100!
h3. 1. Connected Device Detection
 * Given a Falcon headset is connected to the mobile device
 * When the user starts the onboarding flow
 * Then the app detects the connected headset model and cushion configuration.

h3. 2. Correct Images Displayed
 * Given the headset model and cushion type are identified
 * When the relevant onboarding steps are shown
 * Then the images displayed must correspond to the detected Falcon model and cushion.

h3. 3. Consistency During Onboarding
 * Given a Falcon headset remains connected during onboarding
 * When the user progresses through onboarding steps
 * Then the images must remain consistent with the detected headset configuration when started the onboarding (?)(!)

h3. 4. Exception — Device Not Connected
 * Given no Falcon headset is connected to the mobile device
 * When the user starts onboarding
 * Then the app displays the default Falcon onboarding images

h3. 5. Exception — Device Disconnects During Onboarding
 * Given a Falcon headset was initially detected
 * When the device disconnects during onboarding
 * Then the app continues using the last detected model images

h2. Cloud images dependency 🚨
 - Cloud tagged images for all Falcon models with different ear cups

!screenshot-2.png|width=25!

column1: ANDROID 🟥 GREY FALCON | Wearing Preference
column34:
As a Grey Falcon Stereo user, I'd like to be able to set headset microphone boom position to the left or right side to manage volume level, stereo channels or media controls so I can get the most out of my headset.
 * The setting seems to be a toggle in General section on Device Settings screen (to be confirmed).
 * Latest Device UI Spec can be found here: [UI Specifications-Falcon - Documents - UI Specs - All Documents|https://hp.sharepoint.com/teams/UISpecifications-Falcon/Shared%20Documents/Forms/AllItems.aspx?id=%2Fteams%2FUISpecifications%2DFalcon%2FShared%20Documents%2FUI%20Specs&sortField=Modified&isAscending=false&viewid=e64641e0%2D6ad4%2D4d08%2Da90f%2D85e8607b6823&p=true]

 * Global UI Spec: [General - Headset Business Unit - HP R&D Wiki|https://rndwiki.inc.hpicorp.net/confluence/spaces/HBU/pages/1436336540/General]

column1: iOS 🟥 GREY FALCON 🎧 Wearing Preferences
column34:
As a Grey Falcon Stereo user, I'd like to be able to set headset microphone boom position to the left or right side to manage volume level, stereo channels or media controls so I can get the most out of my headset.
 * The setting seems to be a toggle in General section on Device Settings screen (To be confirmed).
 * Latest Device UI Spec can be found here: [UI Specifications-Falcon - Documents - UI Specs - All Documents|https://hp.sharepoint.com/teams/UISpecifications-Falcon/Shared%20Documents/Forms/AllItems.aspx?id=%2Fteams%2FUISpecifications%2DFalcon%2FShared%20Documents%2FUI%20Specs&sortField=Modified&isAscending=false&viewid=e64641e0%2D6ad4%2D4d08%2Da90f%2D85e8607b6823&p=true]
 * As of 3/17 the latest version of Device UI Spec is Falcon_ui_specification_revAv4.pdf. Refer to page 24.
 * Global UI Spec: [General - Headset Business Unit - HP R&D Wiki|https://rndwiki.inc.hpicorp.net/confluence/spaces/HBU/pages/1436336540/General]
 * Figma guidance received from design team: [Studio Mobile Spec – Figma|https://www.figma.com/design/kVruq94TvQDlPCELGmJcs5/Studio-Mobile-Spec?node-id=13700-16986&t=WGL9Wt60uOVnEMRt-0]

!image-2026-03-17-17-01-25-045.png!
!image-2026-03-17-17-40-17-303.png!
column830:
Turns out this is not ready for development - Gray Falcon *does not support* a command to change wearing preference. 

Currently, only setting that would let us change wearing preference on device is 0x0434 - but the description states that it's only for headsets without boom sensor. Gray Falcon has one - we receive events when we change the mic boom position.
This setting is unavailable on Gray Falcon - FW version 252.188.0.5030, DSDK 0.30.0 and 0.31.0 (newest one). 

 !Screenshot 2026-03-24 at 14.12.08.png|width=80%! 

The other possibility would be this deckard ID, but it is just an event and setting, we cannot change it from app side using command. 

 !Screenshot 2026-03-24 at 14.07.58.png|width=80%! 

UI and localizations for this are done on our side, but this needs some clarification.

[~joel.maranhao@hp.com] [~michal.szatkowski@hp.com]

column1: ANDROID 🤖 STUDIO 📐 Re-order intro screen to match figma
column34:
*What*

Re-order intro screen to match figma

*User Story:* As an existing or new end user of Poly Lens mobile, soon to be Studio Mobile, I want to have a consistent experience when installing the application or updating my application and be informed of the new branding and design with a "What's New Modal". 

 

*Acceptance Criteria:*
 # This work is specific for the Android implementation of the mobile app
 # For a new installation of Studio Mobile (5.0.0)
 ## Present Welcome Screen
 ## Pop up the "Allow to send notifications" overlay
 ## Present the EULA (must be accepted to continue)
 ## Present the Login or Continue as guest screen
 ### Login Path
 #### User goes through the log-in experience
 ### Continue as Guest Path
 #### User gets presented with a data consent path (Don't Share or Share)
 ## Once either of those paths are completed we then pop up the "use location consent"
 ## Pop up the "use FaceID" consent
 ## Land on the "Welcome to Poly Studio" page
 ## When the user clicks "Get started" we should take them through the carousel that they can click through or skip.
 ## Now they should be in the app
 # For an update of Poly Lens Mobile to Studio Mobile (5.0.0 or beyond)
 ## A logged in user would experience
 ### Present Welcome Screen
 ### Land on the "Welcome to Poly Studio" page
 ### Now they should be in the app
 ## If the user was a guest
 ### Present Welcome Screen
 ### Present the Login or Continue as guest screen
 #### If they continue as a guest
 ##### User gets presented with a data consent path (Don't Share or Share)
 ##### Land on the "Welcome to Poly Studio" page 
 #### If they change and decide to log-in
 ##### Land on the "Welcome to Poly Studio" page 

 

 

*Figma:* [Studio Mobile Spec – Figma|https://www.figma.com/design/kVruq94TvQDlPCELGmJcs5/Studio-Mobile-Spec?node-id=7414-54655&t=qfW0RVq3AIzjCxW1-0]

 

*Historic Context:*

{*}---{*}{*}{*}
h1. Product Input

Implement What's New modal for Studio Android

*Figma:* [Studio Mobile Spec – Figma|https://www.figma.com/design/kVruq94TvQDlPCELGmJcs5/Studio-Mobile-Spec?node-id=7414-54655&t=qfW0RVq3AIzjCxW1-0]

We should call out the In-App Release notes feature in addition to Quick Settings in the What's New modal. We couldn't call out the feature even though we released it in previous release (v4.7.0) because, for most headsets FW was not available in GraphQL APIs due to missing metadata tags in KaaS. We are working to remediate that and also (as discussed) planning to make app release notes available via GraphQL APIs so we can show those to users in within the app.

column1: ANDROID 🤖 STUDIO 📐 Quick Settings (integration)
column34:
h1. Product Input

*Background*

Quick Settings are shortcuts to certain device settings. They enable users to directly change certain device settings without having to go to Device Settings screen and scrolling to locate the concerned setting. Unlike for most other features, Quick Settings are to change (evolve) quite a bit from Lens to Studio.

*Outline of the Changes*
 * Quick settings are moving out from behind the three-dot icon to have their own real-estate at the front & center of the Home Screen - right below the device image. It is possible that few devices only have User Guide as a Quick Setting option.
 * Quick Settings are no longer fixed - user will have the option of choosing which specific settings they want to have at the home screen.
 * Tapping on the three-dot icon will now take the user directly to the Device Settings screen.
 * In Device Settings, user will see a new option. This new option is called {color:#0747a6}*Quick Settings Selection*{color}. On tapping this option, users will be taken to a new screen where they will be able to select {color:#0747a6}*up to 3*{color} device settings to appear as Quick Settings on Home Screen. If they try to select more, a modal will be displayed to remind them about it.
 * On the Home Screen, when a user taps on a Quick Setting shortcut, a module similar to the App Support CTA on Lens UI will open. It will have all the settings that are available on the Device Settings page for that group of settings for the concerned device.

*Acceptance Criteria*
 * 
 ** The first 3 items in the *Deep Settings,* AFTER *Software Update,* will always be the 3 default ship state *Quick Settings* for all devices, legacy and new.
 ** -When a user updates the app, they'll see that the default quick settings for their device have now moved to their new position from under the three-dot menu. And, by default, the quick settings toggle in {color:#0747a6}Device Settings > Quick Setting Selection{color} screen will be in enabled state.-
 ** -The no. of default quick settings for a device could be 0, 1, 2 or 3. the device has any qualifying quick setting shortcut-
 ** -The default Quick Settings for all devices will remain same with the exception of the below two quick settings:-
 *** -+Gear icon+ that takes the user to Device Settings screen: +This quick setting needs to be removed for all devices+-
 *** -+Trash Bin icon+ for deleting a device:  +This quick setting needs to be removed for all devices+-
 ** -This will _+likely+_ mean that for all devices except Falcon & Free20/60/60+, when users update the app to 5.0.0, they would _+likely+_ see User Guide as the only default Quick Setting. For Free20/60/60+, the users will not see any quick settings. users will see Tutorials as the only default Quick Setting. {color:#0747a6}-*[09/25 Update:* Looks like the 'Tutorials' grouping of User-Guide/Fit-Test/QSG/etc. has been retired and these individual settings have since been moved to Device Settings. In that case, default Quick Settings for Free20/60/60+ will change. Confirmation awaited from Kem.\\\\\\\\\\\{*}]*-{color}-
 ** -While Falcon currently has DND as one of the default quick settings, the new default Quick Setting for Falcon would be-
 *** -Quick Start Guide-
 *** -Equalizer-
 *** -ANC-
 * When user adds a new device on the updated app, the default Quick Settings will appear in their new position.
 * By default, the Quick Settings toggle on the {color:#0747a6}*Quick Settings Selection*{color} screen will be enabled.
 * When a user taps on a Quick Setting, the Quick Setting 'module' opens up. User can adjust the settings and tap anywhere outside the 'module' to go back to Home Screen.
 * When user clicks on the the three-dot menu, they are taken to the Device Settings screen.
 * All Device Settings should continue to work as they do currently whether accessed through Quick Settings or Device Settings screen.
 * No quick settings will be shown when device is disconnected.
 * Quick access 'tap-off' cards will need to be created for all applicable groups of settings available in Deep Settings on Device Settings screen. {color:#0747a6}*<Update 09/29: Ujjaval to add indicative list of such settings here>*{color}.

*Figma*

[Studio Mobile Spec – Figma|https://www.figma.com/design/kVruq94TvQDlPCELGmJcs5/Studio-Mobile-Spec?node-id=844-25053&t=JGn5sAt51SGdhFVb-0]

 

*Notes:*

In a future release, targeting 5.1, we will determine a more programmatic way to establish the default quick settings.
h1. Engineering Input

As user, I want to {color:#4c9aff}quickly access my favorite settings{color} from the device home screen
h4. Acceptance Criteria
 * Up to 3 quick settings
 * when it is a device settings then show dynamic bottom sheet (~70% of the screen, to-be-discussed between iOS and Android)
 * when it is a tutorial, guide, fit test, eq etc... then the app takes the user to that flow
 * -do NOT show DFU on the selector-
 * do NOT show Delete Device on the selector

TO BE CONFIRMED
 * -for all *legacy product* the first 3 items in the *Deep Settings* will always be the 3 default ship state {*}Quick Settings{*}.-
 * -for *new product* (Falcon) UX/PM will determine what the 3 default ship state Quick Settings will be-
 ** -method should be the same between legacy and new product-

column1: ANDROID 🤖 STUDIO 📐 Device Settings when device connected
column34:
h1. Product Input

*Background*
While there are no changes in the settings available for a device, the user-flow of Device Settings screen in Studio is a little different from its avatar in Lens UI. In Lens UI, the Device Settings screen could not be accessed at all when device was disconnected. Whereas, in Studio UI, even if the device is disconnected, tapping on the three-dot menu icon will bring the user over to Device Settings screen where users will be able to access resources such as Onboarding, User Guide, Quick Start Guide & the option to delete the device from the Studio mobile app.

Resources available to users when device is disconnected would vary from device to device. While most devices will have user guides and all devices would show the option to delete the device, few devices such as Free20/60/Legend 30/50, will also have Tutorial options enabled.

*Who, What, Why*
As Lens Mobile product manager, I want the look & feel of the Lens Mobile app to be updated to Studio UI so that HP Poly Studio Mobile users have consistent UX & brand experience across various HP Poly Studio apps. 

*Acceptance Criteria*
 * Update the Device Screen in Lens mobile UI to align with Studio UI design as outlined in Figma.
 * [Figma|https://www.figma.com/design/kVruq94TvQDlPCELGmJcs5/Studio-Mobile-Spec?node-id=844-25053&m=dev]
 * The sequence of settings for a device will remain the same.

*Out of Scope*
Quick Settings related changes (tracked in a separate ticket).


h1. Engineering Input

h3. *DEEP* SETTINGS ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
 # hybrid approach to settings (mixing App features, Devices and Tutorial) as describes in Figma (Nikita) vs *follow* Global Spec device settings order
 ** follow global spec and then show app settings > Kem/Nikita to align Figma 
 # *when* device is connected *then* do not show Delete Device


h3. Docs
 * [Studio Mobile Rebrand / Device Menu |https://www.figma.com/design/kVruq94TvQDlPCELGmJcs5/Studio-Mobile-Spec?node-id=844-25053&m=dev]

h2. New Features
h3. IDUN Music 2 v7 - Spatial audio support

* Firmware (Unencrypted): [Poly_Focus_V0252.1132.0.9934_unencrypted.zip|https://polyrepo.poly.com/artifactory/Professional_Headsets/DevTest/falcon-poly/feature/HSFWCH-9564-falcon-spatial-ui--ux-integr/9934/Unencrypted/P35690-001_PUBLISH_FILE_HP_Poly_Focus_V0252.1132.0.9934_unencrypted.zip]
 * Firmware (Encrypted): [Poly_Focus_V0252.1132.0.9934.zip|https://polyrepo.poly.com/artifactory/Professional_Headsets/DevTest/falcon-poly/feature/HSFWCH-9564-falcon-spatial-ui--ux-integr/9934/Encrypted/P35690-001_PUBLISH_FILE_HP_Poly_Focus_V0252.1132.0.9934.zip]
 * Companion App:
 ** ​​[poly_qcc-skin_poly-android-24.apk|https://hp-my.sharepoint.com/:u:/p/david_hudson1/IQDmGkjqrhGdQq7RQFx1zABpAZ1-NWSyPB4F6-UaEJw3aVc]
 ** ​​[poly_qcc-skin_poly-android-24.apk|https://cloud.unity.com/public-share/build-automation/share?shareId=hnAAJRsglbHMRQ5G171hf6v4CH1xo6W7PDsbow1O4nw]

h2. User Experience Enhancements
h3. Audio Chain Management
 * Music Chain: Default (Poly) configuration with toggleable IDUN spatial audio.
 * Scene Support: IDUN mode exclusively supports music playback.

h3. Controls
 * Volume Wheel: Medium press (>2 seconds) toggles between Poly and IDUN modes and enables/disables spatial audio.
 * Audio Feedback: Tone notifications confirm feature activation/deactivation.
 * Spatial Audio Voice Prompts: Not available in this release.

h2. Technical Implementation
h3. Processing Architecture
 * Single-Core Operation: IDUN music chain optimized for P0 processor execution.
 * Volume Calibration: Pending evaluation. 

h3. Device Integration
 * Android Companion App: Full-featured IDUN control interface with real-time visualization.
 * Lens/Poly Tuning tool: Headset supports Spatial Audio Deckard messages; current tools do not implement them.
 * Connectivity: Bluetooth GAIA and Bladerunner protocol support.
 * Settings Persistence: Feature states maintained across device restarts.

h3. Known Items
 * No bug fixes included in this release.;;;

column1: ANDROID 🤖 User Consent FTU Improvements
column34:
*User Story:* As a Product Manager, I want to be able to capture as much data about my end users as possible as possible so I can improve my decision making around what to add, remove or improve in the new Poly Studio mobile application and need to include the data consent option during FTU to drive more user opt-in selections.

*Background*

The count of serial no. of unique connected devices from Google Analytics events doesn't seem to be very high when contrasted with headset sales figures and Lens connected devices (going into millions of devices). Even with 25% data consent ratio (based on App Store analytics, the count of unique devices from GA events stands at ~40K in a month's time. This warrants root cause analysis and remediation.

-*Solution to Explore*-
 * -Estimate current consent ratio from GA events data.-
 * -Review GA event recording scenarios to make sure any fundamental scenarios are not missed-
 * -Calculate overlap between GA device s. no. and logged in devices-
 * -Show data consent form when app is updated to 5.0.0 as a one-time exception-
 * -Get a more engaging design for data consent modal-
 * -Update the copy on the modal to emphasize that the anonymized data being collected is never used for sales purposes in any way shape or form. Also shall we say that we don't store your email as part of this anonymized data collection.-

*AC:* 
 * Provide the ability to either log-in or continue as a guest user
 * If the end user logs-in they automatically consent to sharing data by logging in
 ** There should be no option to stop sharing their data unless they log out and continue as a guest
 * If the end user continues as a guest
 ** Show data consent form when app is updated to 5.0.0 as a one-time exception
 *** The consent form is consistent with what is shown in Studio Desktop FTU.
 *** After the release of 5.0.0 the data consent form should only appear after the first install and during FTU
 ** There should be an option to share or not share (stop sharing) their data under their settings -> account

*Designs:*
 * Coming soon

column1: iOS  STUDIO 📐 App Onboarding (What's New)
column34:
*User Story:* As an existing or new end user of Poly Lens mobile, soon to be Studio Mobile, I want to have a consistent experience when installing the application or updating my application and be informed of the new branding and design with a "What's New Modal". 

 

*Acceptance Criteria:*
 # This work is specific for the iOS implementation of the mobile app
 # For a new installation of Studio Mobile (5.0.0)
 ## Present Welcome Screen
 ## Pop up the "Allow to send notifications" overlay
 ## Present the EULA (must be accepted to continue)
 ## Present the Login or Continue as guest screen
 ### Login Path
 #### User goes through the log-in experience
 ### Continue as Guest Path
 #### User gets presented with a data consent path (Don't Share or Share)
 ## Once either of those paths are completed we then pop up the "use location consent"
 ## Pop up the "use FaceID" consent
 ## Land on the "Welcome to Poly Studio" page
 ## When the user clicks "Get started" we should take them through the carousel that they can click through or skip.
 ## Now they should be in the app
 # For an update of Poly Lens Mobile to Studio Mobile (5.0.0 or beyond)
 ## A logged in user would experience
 ### Present Welcome Screen
 ### Land on the "Welcome to Poly Studio" page
 ### Now they should be in the app
 ## If the user was a guest
 ### Present Welcome Screen
 ### Present the Login or Continue as guest screen
 #### If they continue as a guest
 ##### User gets presented with a data consent path (Don't Share or Share)
 ##### Land on the "Welcome to Poly Studio" page 
 #### If they change and decide to log-in
 ##### Land on the "Welcome to Poly Studio" page 

 

 

*Figma:* [Studio Mobile Spec – Figma|https://www.figma.com/design/kVruq94TvQDlPCELGmJcs5/Studio-Mobile-Spec?node-id=7414-54655&t=qfW0RVq3AIzjCxW1-0]

 

*Historic Context:*
----
h1. Product Input

Implement What's New modal for Studio Android

*Figma:* [Studio Mobile Spec – Figma|https://www.figma.com/design/kVruq94TvQDlPCELGmJcs5/Studio-Mobile-Spec?node-id=7414-54655&t=qfW0RVq3AIzjCxW1-0]

[~brian.marris@hp.com] We should call out the In-App Release notes feature in addition to Quick Settings in the What's New modal. We couldn't call out the feature even though we released it in previous release (v4.7.0) because, for most headsets FW was not available in GraphQL APIs due to missing metadata tags in KaaS. We are working to remediate that and also (as discussed) planning to make app release notes available via GraphQL APIs so we can show those to users in within the app.

column1623:
4/Nov/25 7:55 AM;damian.woroszyl@hp.com;Hi [~brian.marris@hp.com], cc [~joel.maranhao@hp.com] [~ujjaval.gupta@hp.com] 

The current order of screens and consents in the app is quite different from what is currently in acceptance criteria. Do we really want to change the order in scope of this task, or do we just want to add the 'welcome to poly studio' in a fitting place? 

For new installation the current order of steps in the app is:
1. Present Welcome Screen (splash screen)
2. EULA screen
3. Bluetooth permission (dialog on EULA screen)
– welcome to poly studio here? –
4. The welcome carousel that user can skip through
5. At the end of carousel user can opt to location consent (dialog)
6. Login or Continue as guest screen + notification consent on entry (dialog)
6.1. If user selected guest - ask for data consent
7. user is on home screen

 

So -  login/guest selection is in different place, and all consents are also in different places.

My recommendation would be to only add the new screen and not change order of what we have currently in the app

column1: ANDROID 🤖 STUDIO 📐 Find My Device
column34:
h1. Product Input

*Who, What, Why*
As Lens Mobile product manager, I want the look & feel of the Lens Mobile app to be updated to Studio UI so that HP Poly Studio Mobile users have consistent UX & brand experience across various HP Poly Studio apps. 

*Acceptance Criteria*
 * Update the Find My Device UI to match with Studio UI design as outlined in Figma.
 * The map style/colors will remain same even though they seem to have been changed in Figma.
 * Figma: [Studio Mobile Rebrand / Illustrations / DFU|https://www.figma.com/design/kVruq94TvQDlPCELGmJcs5/Studio-Mobile-Spec?node-id=847-26843&m=dev]

h1. Engineering Input
h2. User Story

As a user, I want to be able to find the localization of my headset from the Lens Mobile app General Menu
h2. Acceptance Criteria
h3. Scenario-oriented Acceptance
h4. device is connected / play the "find me" tone
 * {color:#4c9aff}*Given*{color} my headset is connected to my phone
 * {color:#de350b}*When*{color} I tap the General menu (top left)
 ** And tap Find My Device
 ** And tap the device I want to locate
 * {color:#00875a}*Then*{color} a drawer open from the bottom, saying the my device (name) is Connected
 * {color:#de350b}*When*{color} I tap the "Play" button
 * {color:#00875a}*Then*{color} I am prompted with a "Warning! Exposure to high sound level"
 * {color:#de350b}*When*{color} I tap "Ok got it"
 ** And Tap the "Play" button again
 * {color:#00875a}*Then*{color} the app sends a command to my headset
 ** And my headset starts emitting a beeping tone
 * {color:#de350b}*When*{color} I tap on the "Stop" (Square) icon
 * {color:#00875a}*Then*{color} the sound stops

h3. rule-based acceptance
 * for connected device, you can play the "Find Me" tone
 * for disconnected you can't play the "Find Me" tone, you can only see the last connected date and the percentage recorded at that time
 * the blue circle on the map shows the location of your phone recorded when headset was last time connected (location access must be granted)
 * show grayed-out pin for the disconnected device

 !screenshot-1.png|width=75%! 

{+}Note{+}: Bluetooth connection range falls under the same threshold as the GPS accuracy, phone's location is treated as the headset location

h2. Readiness Gaps 🚨
 * Screen with Device list - Not a blocker but to clarify
 * Ask if any specific scenario expected for earbuds ? - Not a blocker but to clarify

column1: ANDROID 🟢 View Studio App Release Notes inside the App
column34:
An an end-user I would like to access -both my- Lens Mobile -and Device Firmware- release notes within the app, rather than being redirected to an external URL{-}, before I proceed with updating my app or device.{-}
h2. Cloud side work
 # Studio app needs to be listed under the models in products-seeder service with an SKU.
 # At the time of publishing to KaaS, the release notes must be tagged with the right version and the right SKU

column1: ANDROID 🤖 STUDIO 📐 Home Screen (except Quick Settings)
column34:
h1. Product input

*Who, What, Why*
As Lens Mobile product manager, I want the look & feel of the Lens Mobile app to be updated to Studio UI so that HP Poly Studio Mobile users have consistent UX & brand experience across various HP Poly Studio apps.

*Acceptance Criteria*
 * All existing capabilities and features carry over from Lens to Studio (except Quick Settings).
 * Update the UI of Home screen (Device Status screen) in Lens mobile app to match Studio UI design as outlined in Figma.
 * Figma: [Studio Mobile Spec – Figma|https://www.figma.com/design/kVruq94TvQDlPCELGmJcs5/Studio-Mobile-Spec?node-id=506-8495&t=9MAujgZXAlKbgJ62-0]

*Out of Scope*
Quick Settings related changes outlined in Figma are tracked in separate tickets.
h1. Engineering input
h3. User Stories

As a *user* ...
 * I need to be informed about my *{color:#4c9aff}device’s battery status{color}* so I can avoid running out of power during a call or while using media.
 * I want to be notified when a *{color:#4c9aff}recent update is available{color}* so I can take advantage of the latest features.
 * I want to view the *{color:#4c9aff}firmware release notes{color}* to understand the most recent improvements.

h4. Rule-based Acceptance Criteria
 * Battery icons must align with [General Global UI spec|https://rndwiki.inc.hpicorp.net/confluence/spaces/HBU/pages/1436336540/General] on Confluence and [Battery status|https://www.figma.com/design/bTBoWeajYswVFRQwinCW8e/Lens-Mobile-UI-Library?node-id=4047-961&t=e5ZopJtsKZpufznT-4] on Figma
 * Status follows different configuration depending on the device connected:
 ** headset + charge case (flamingo)
 ** earbud left/right + charge case (opsrey, emu)
 ** headset only
 * Show "Software Update" button when update available
 * Present the firmware version which links to the firmware release notes
 * App implements Dark mode (!)

h4. Scenario-Oriented Acceptance (Given, When, Then)

Describe particular user interactions using GWT or UML diagrams
Follow [Guidelines and Examples|https://github.azc.ext.hp.com/Poly-Lens-Apps/Lens-App-iOS/wiki/Use-case-scenario-vs-User-Story]
h4. Readiness Gaps 🚨
 - Missing Figma for when Flamingo is connected (!)
 - No label for Flamingo e.g. R for right, L for left, C for case (in any language?), what's the label for a simple Headset with no L or R (?) (!)

h4. Out-of-scope
 * *Quick Settings* are addressed in a dedicated ticket

h4. Docs
 * [Studio Mobile Rebrand / Status Box |https://www.figma.com/design/kVruq94TvQDlPCELGmJcs5/Studio-Mobile-Spec?node-id=506-8495&m=dev]

column1: iOS  STUDIO 📐 Quick Settings (integration)
column34:
h1. Product Input

*Background*

Quick Settings are shortcuts to certain device settings. They enable users to directly change certain device settings without having to go to Device Settings screen and scrolling to locate the concerned setting. Unlike for most other features, Quick Settings are to change (evolve) quite a bit from Lens to Studio.

*Outline of the Changes*
 * Quick settings are moving out from behind the three-dot icon to have their own real-estate at the front & center of the Home Screen - right below the device image. It is possible that few devices only have User Guide as a Quick Setting option.
 * Quick Settings are no longer fixed - user will have the option of choosing which specific settings they want to have at the home screen.
 * Tapping on the three-dot icon will now take the user directly to the Device Settings screen.
 * In Device Settings, user will see a new option. This new option is called {color:#0747a6}*Quick Settings Selection*{color}. On tapping this option, users will be taken to a new screen where they will be able to select {color:#0747a6}*up to 3*{color} device settings to appear as Quick Settings on Home Screen. If they try to select more, a modal will be displayed to remind them about it.
 * On the Home Screen, when a user taps on a Quick Setting shortcut, a module similar to the App Support CTA on Lens UI will open. It will have all the settings that are available on the Device Settings page for that group of settings for the concerned device.

*Acceptance Criteria*
 * The first 3 items in the *Deep Settings,* AFTER *Software Update,* will always be the 3 default ship state *Quick Settings* for all devices, legacy and new.
 * -When a user updates the app, they'll see that the default quick settings for their device have now moved to their new position from under the three-dot menu. And, by default, the quick settings toggle in {color:#0747a6}Device Settings > Quick Setting Selection{color} screen will be in enabled state.-
 * -The no. of default quick settings for a device could be 0, 1, 2 or 3. the device has any qualifying quick setting shortcut-
 * -The default Quick Settings for all devices will remain same with the exception of the below two quick settings:-
 ** -+Gear icon+ that takes the user to Device Settings screen: +This quick setting needs to be removed for all devices+-
 ** -+Trash Bin icon+ for deleting a device:  +This quick setting needs to be removed for all devices+-

 * -This will _+likely+_ mean that for all devices except Falcon & Free20/60/60+, when users update the app to 5.0.0, they would _+likely+_ see User Guide as the only default Quick Setting. For Free20/60/60+, the users will not see any quick settings. users will see Tutorials as the only default Quick Setting. {color:#0747a6}-*[09/25 Update:* Looks like the 'Tutorials' grouping of User-Guide/Fit-Test/QSG/etc. has been retired and these individual settings have since been moved to Device Settings. In that case, default Quick Settings for Free20/60/60+ will change. Confirmation awaited from Kem.\\\\\\\\\\{*}]*-{color}-
 * -While Falcon currently has DND as one of the default quick settings, the new default Quick Setting for Falcon would be-
 ** -Quick Start Guide-
 ** -Equalizer-
 ** -ANC-
 * When user adds a new device on the updated app, the default Quick Settings will appear in their new position.
 * By default, the Quick Settings toggle on the {color:#0747a6}*Quick Settings Selection*{color} screen will be enabled.
 * When a user taps on a Quick Setting, the Quick Setting 'module' opens up. User can adjust the settings and tap anywhere outside the 'module' to go back to Home Screen.
 * When user clicks on the three-dot menu, they are taken to the Device Settings screen.
 * All Device Settings should continue to work as they do currently whether accessed through Quick Settings or Device Settings screen.
 * No quick settings will be shown when device is disconnected.
 * Quick access 'tap-off' cards will need to be created for all applicable groups of settings available in Deep Settings on Device Settings screen. {color:#0747a6}*<Update 09/29: Ujjaval to add indicative list of such settings here>*{color}.

*Figma*

[Studio Mobile Spec – Figma|https://www.figma.com/design/kVruq94TvQDlPCELGmJcs5/Studio-Mobile-Spec?node-id=844-25053&t=JGn5sAt51SGdhFVb-0]

 

*Notes:*

In a future release, targeting 5.1, we will determine a more programmatic way to establish the default quick settings.
h1. Engineering Input

As user, I want to {color:#4c9aff}quickly access my favorite settings{color} from the device home screen
h4. Acceptance Criteria
 * Up to 3 quick settings
 * when it is a device settings then show dynamic bottom sheet (~70% of the screen, to-be-discussed between iOS and Android)
 * when it is a tutorial, guide, fit test, eq etc... then the app takes the user to that flow
 * -do NOT show DFU on the selector-
 * do NOT show Delete Device on the selector

TO BE CONFIRMED
 * -for all *legacy product* the first 3 items in the *Deep Settings* will always be the 3 default ship state {*}Quick Settings{*}.-
 * -for *new product* (Falcon) UX/PM will determine what the 3 default ship state Quick Settings will be-
 ** -method should be the same between legacy and new product-

 * Figma: [Studio Mobile Spec – Figma|https://www.figma.com/design/kVruq94TvQDlPCELGmJcs5/Studio-Mobile-Spec?node-id=506-8495&t=9MAujgZXAlKbgJ62-0]

*Out of Scope*
Quick Settings related changes outlined in Figma are tracked in separate tickets.
h1. Engineering input
h3. User Stories

As a *user* ...
 * I need to be informed about my *{color:#4c9aff}device’s battery status{color}* so I can avoid running out of power during a call or while using media.
 * I want to be notified when a *{color:#4c9aff}recent update is available{color}* so I can take advantage of the latest features.
 * I want to view the *{color:#4c9aff}firmware release notes{color}* to understand the most recent improvements.

h4. Rule-based Acceptance Criteria
 * Battery icons must align with [General Global UI spec|https://rndwiki.inc.hpicorp.net/confluence/spaces/HBU/pages/1436336540/General] on Confluence and [Battery status|https://www.figma.com/design/bTBoWeajYswVFRQwinCW8e/Lens-Mobile-UI-Library?node-id=4047-961&t=e5ZopJtsKZpufznT-4] on Figma
 * Status follows different configuration depending on the device connected:
 ** headset + charge case (flamingo)
 ** earbud left/right + charge case (opsrey, emu)
 ** headset only
 * Show "Software Update" button when update available
 * Present the firmware version which links to the firmware release notes
 * App implements Dark mode (!)

h4. Scenario-Oriented Acceptance (Given, When, Then)

Describe particular user interactions using GWT or UML diagrams
Follow [Guidelines and Examples|https://github.azc.ext.hp.com/Poly-Lens-Apps/Lens-App-iOS/wiki/Use-case-scenario-vs-User-Story]
h4. Readiness Gaps 🚨
 - Missing Figma for when Flamingo is connected (!)
 - No label for Flamingo e.g. R for right, L for left, C for case (in any language?), what's the label for a simple Headset with no L or R (?) (!)

h4. Out-of-scope
 * *Quick Settings* are addressed in a dedicated ticket

h4. Docs
 * [Studio Mobile Rebrand / Status Box |https://www.figma.com/design/kVruq94TvQDlPCELGmJcs5/Studio-Mobile-Spec?node-id=506-8495&m=dev]

column1: iOS  STUDIO 📐 Find My Device
column34:
h1. Product Input

*Who, What, Why*
As Lens Mobile product manager, I want the look & feel of the Lens Mobile app to be updated to Studio UI so that HP Poly Studio Mobile users have consistent UX & brand experience across various HP Poly Studio apps. 

*Acceptance Criteria*
 * Update the Find My Device UI to match with Studio UI design as outlined in Figma.
 * The map style/colors will remain same even though they seem to have been changed in Figma.
 * Figma: [Studio Mobile Rebrand / Illustrations / DFU|https://www.figma.com/design/kVruq94TvQDlPCELGmJcs5/Studio-Mobile-Spec?node-id=847-26843&m=dev]

h1. Engineering Input
h2. User Story

As a user, I want to be able to find the localization of my headset from the Lens Mobile app General Menu

Following discussions with the UX team, the core functionality of FMD remains unchanged. The scope of the rebranding effort includes:
 * Updating FMD illustrations to align with Figma
 * Modifying the annotation pin to match Figma.
 * Applying updated font styles and color schemes according to Figma

h2. Acceptance Criteria
h3. Scenario-oriented Acceptance
h4. device is connected / play the "find me" tone
 * {color:#4c9aff}*Given*{color} my headset is connected to my phone
 * {color:#de350b}*When*{color} I tap the General menu (top left)
 ** And tap Find My Device
 ** And tap the device I want to locate
 * {color:#00875a}*Then*{color} a drawer open from the bottom, saying the my device (name) is Connected
 * {color:#de350b}*When*{color} I tap the "Play" button
 * {color:#00875a}*Then*{color} I am prompted with a "Warning! Exposure to high sound level"
 * {color:#de350b}*When*{color} I tap "Ok got it"
 ** And Tap the "Play" button again
 * {color:#00875a}*Then*{color} the app sends a command to my headset
 ** And my headset starts emitting a beeping tone
 * {color:#de350b}*When*{color} I tap on the "Stop" (Square) icon
 * {color:#00875a}*Then*{color} the sound stops

h3. rule-based acceptance
 * for connected device, you can play the "Find Me" tone
 * for disconnected you can't play the "Find Me" tone, you can only see the last connected date and the percentage recorded at that time
 * the blue circle on the map shows the location of your phone recorded when headset was last time connected (location access must be granted)
 * show grayed-out pin for the disconnected device
 * map annotations should no longer be Apple Map defaults but should align to Figma design

!screenshot-1.png|width=75!

{+}Note{+}: Bluetooth connection range falls under the same threshold as the GPS accuracy, phone's location is treated as the headset location
h2. Readiness Gaps 🚨
 * Screen with Device list - Not a blocker but to clarify
 * Ask if any specific scenario expected for earbuds ? - Not a blocker but to clarify

column1: iOS  STUDIO 📐 Device Settings when device connected
column34:
h1. Product Input

*Background*
While there are no changes in the settings available for a device, the user-flow of Device Settings screen in Studio is a little different from its avatar in Lens UI. In Lens UI, the Device Settings screen could not be accessed at all when device was disconnected. Whereas, in Studio UI, even if the device is disconnected, tapping on the three-dot menu icon will bring the user over to Device Settings screen where users will be able to access resources such as Onboarding, User Guide, Quick Start Guide & the option to delete the device from the Studio mobile app.

Resources available to users when device is disconnected would vary from device to device. While most devices will have user guides and all devices would show the option to delete the device, few devices such as Free20/60/Legend 30/50, will also have Tutorial options enabled.

*Who, What, Why*
As Lens Mobile product manager, I want the look & feel of the Lens Mobile app to be updated to Studio UI so that HP Poly Studio Mobile users have consistent UX & brand experience across various HP Poly Studio apps. 

*Acceptance Criteria*
 * Update the Device Screen in Lens mobile UI to align with Studio UI design as outlined in Figma.
 * [Figma|https://www.figma.com/design/kVruq94TvQDlPCELGmJcs5/Studio-Mobile-Spec?node-id=844-25053&m=dev]
 * The sequence of settings for a device will remain the same.

*Out of Scope*
Quick Settings related changes (tracked in a separate ticket).


h1. Engineering Input

h3. *DEEP* SETTINGS ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
 # hybrid approach to settings (mixing App features, Devices and Tutorial) as describes in Figma (Nikita) vs *follow* Global Spec device settings order
 ** follow global spec and then show app settings > Kem/Nikita to align Figma 
 # *when* device is connected *then* do not show Delete Device

column1: ANDROID 🚨 CTAs & Copy on the Poly Product Improvement Program screen
column34:
h2. Who, What, Why

As Poly Lens product manager, I want to ensure that Poly Lens Android app is compliant with applicable privacy laws (such as GDPR & California Consumer Privacy Act (US) et al.). In the new GA events tracking we'll be recording device serial number for certain GA events. Device serial number is generally considered as personal information. Therefore, to be able to record device serial numbers while remaining compliant with applicable privacy laws, we need to make few changes to the UI wherein we ask for user consent for recording GA events.
h2. Acceptance Criteria
 # Implement changes per the new Figma design on Share Data (Poly Product Improvement Program) screen.
 # Implement changes per the new Figma design on the App Settings screen.

column1: iOS ⚠️ Incoming call when DFU is already underway
column34:
h2. Figma: [Studio Mobile Spec – Figma|https://www.figma.com/design/kVruq94TvQDlPCELGmJcs5/Studio-Mobile-Spec?node-id=506-75253&t=hINQKVO0tgx51j0C-0]
h2. User Story - Who What Why

-As a user, I want the DFU feature in Lens Mobile to be blocked or cancelled during a call so that my headset doesn't disconnect and interrupt the conversation.-
h3. Rule-based Acceptance Criteria
 * *I{-}nterrupt DFU if a call is received{-}*
 ** -Show message explaining why-
 ** -LMW-1321 LMW-1322-

h3. Scenario-based Acceptance Criteria
 # *Given* -my headset is connected to my mobile-
 ** -And a DFU is in progress-
 # *When* -I respond to an incoming call-
 # *Then* -the Lens App interrupts the DFU-
 ** -And show a message explaining why-

h2. Business Requirements

To complete DFU, the device needs to reboot. Therefore, a mid-call reboot will definitely interrupt a call and diminish UX for headset and Lens Mobile user.

*Acceptance criteria*
 * Once DFU begins display relevant guidance to the user below the DFU progress bar
 * -Prevent the user from begin DFU if the device is on a call. Show appropriate message to the user-
 * -If user receives a call while DFU process is underway, the DFU process should stop and the user should be shown appropriate message. *-

column1: iOS  FALCON | Updated Custom Button UX
column34:
*Background*
 * In this story, we need to update the Teams custom button functionality we already released on Lens Mobile apps in previous release v4.6.1 
 * The Lens UX for Teams Custom button has been updated in consultation with Alliance & headset FW teams. The updated UX is documented on Figma here: [Studio Mobile Spec – Figma|https://www.figma.com/design/kVruq94TvQDlPCELGmJcs5/Studio-Mobile-Spec?node-id=506-45427&t=yBgMszQhok3FxLe6-0]
 * However, FW is not yet ready to support those changes. Therefore, in the interim, FW team has provided FW version hardcoded to only return Microsoft Teams as an option for the custom button. In theory, this is supposed to allow us to achieve the Figma designs. However, during the course of implementation, dev team might uncover use cases that can only be addressed once final FW version becomes available.

*Who, What, Why*

As Lens Mobile product manager, I want to make sure that whenever a Falcon headset returns Teams as the only option available for the Teams custom button, Lens Mobile does not allow users to assign any other action to the custom button so that Lens UX is always in sync with headset firmware behavior
 * FW behavior in this case is driven by HP Poly's alliance with Microsoft.
 * Even after user selects Other Providers, as soon as headset can once again detect Teams app, it will automatically switch back to Teams only option (i.e. no other action will be available via custom button). 

*Acceptance Criteria*

Match the Mobile app experience to the UI captured in the updated Figma designs:
 # Update the label for Other Providers and the guidance text below it. It has been updated for both scenarios - when Teams is connected and when its not.
 # Disable the Other Providers option when headset returns Microsoft Teams as the only custom button option.
 # When headset FW returns other actions as available, user can still select Teams. Add a new modal that is triggered when Teams is not connected but the user still switches back from Other Providers to Teams.
 # If a user specifies a custom action in Other Providers, Lens Mobile needs to remember the selected option and when headset is no longer in Teams mode, Lens will show the user had selected previously (before the headset switched to Teams mode by itself)

*NOTE:* Attached screenshots are only meant to serve as examples. Please refer to Figma for latest & exhaustive UI design.

!image-2025-07-22-16-24-42-549.png|width=235,height=487!!image-2025-07-22-16-25-06-210.png|width=228,height=488!!image-2025-07-22-16-31-50-260.png|width=243,height=488!!image-2025-07-22-16-35-02-909.png|width=233,height=488!

column1623:
23/Jul/25 2:52 PM;mateusz.wojtowicz@hp.com;Do we really want to save the user's non-MS Teams choice on our end? This is something we never do for any of the settings. Since the change of the selected option is FW-driven meaning - when MS Teams is detected FW sends us an event that only MS Teams is available at the moment and I guess FW should also send us an event that MS Teams is the selected option. If FW no longer detects MS Teams then it should send us both events again. One informing about all available options and the other one indicating the selected one. 

Keep in mind that a device with custom button can be connected to another device which does not have MS Teams and using that device the option can be changed to anything else. Each device that connects to that headset would store the information about selected option itself and each can be different. However, there is only one option selected and it is stored on the headset itself and therefore the headset should be the source of truth.

Nonetheless, I would suggest to size this story to 5 SP.

 ;;;

column1: iOS  Do NOT allow DFU during a call
column34:
h2. User story - Who What Why

As a user, I want the DFU feature in Lens Mobile to be blocked during a call so that my headset doesn't disconnect and interrupt the conversation.

h3. Rule-based acceptance criteria

 * *Block DFU initiation during a call*
 ** Show message explaining why
 ** LMW-1219 LMW-1220

h3. GWT acceptance criteria
 # *Given* my headset is connected to my mobile
 ** And I am in a call
 # *When* I attempt to attempt to update firmware from the Lens App
 # *Then* the Lens App doesn't allow it 
 ** And shows a message explaining why

h2. Business requirement


Do not allow headset user to begin device firmware update if the device is in a call. To complete DFU, the device needs to reboot. Therefore, a mid-call reboot will definitely interrupt a call and diminish UX for headset and Lens Mobile user.

[Studio Mobile Spec – Figma|https://www.figma.com/design/kVruq94TvQDlPCELGmJcs5/Studio-Mobile-Spec?node-id=3624-24437&t=iVyjNCgES7mgzwGN-0]

h3. Acceptance criteria
 * Prevent the user from begin DFU if the device is on a call. Show appropriate message to the user
 * If user receives a call while DFU process is underway, the DFU process should stop and the user should be shown appropriate message. *{color:#DE350B}OUT-OF-SCOPE OF THIS TICKET{color}*. Splits to LMW-1322 ⛔

column1: ANDROID 🤖 Do NOT allow DFU during a call
column34:
In the DFU process the headset has to reboot and therefore it is momentarily disconnected from the mobile. Therefore, if the user is in a call and initiates DFU, their call would end unexpectedly leading to poor UX with the device and Mobile app.

*Acceptance Criteria*
 * User must not be able to start DFU while the concerned device is in a call.
 * User should be informed that they're not allowed to update device firmware during a call via appropriate message

*Figma*
[https://www.figma.com/design/kVruq94TvQDlPCELGmJcs5/Studio-Mobile-Spec?node-id=3624-24437&t=IGkZj8eDfOnR8ySO-4]

column1: ANDROID | Pre-Release Device Support
column34:
*Who*
Android dev team

*What*
Show information on home screen regarding current device support state based on information from Cloud query and scenarios described in the epic.

Note: support state should be described as an enum and be kept as state in DeviceInfo/HubDevice. The enum should be determined in DeviceCloudDataMapper based on requirements described in epic. The state should be nullable in case we can't determine it.

We already fetch product details when needed, so only modify existing request to read new data (this might cause compilation problems until the fields are ready on all cloud environments)

Additionally: setup hardcoded (constants in code) feature flags for scenarios 5 and 6

*Why*
Refer to the epic

 

*Note:*
You will need to update schema.json files in cloud module to be able to use new fields. There is some information how to do it in our projects README

column1: iOS 💡 Improve Accessibility of Key App Settings in Poly Lens Menu
column34:
As a *Lens Mobile app user*, I want to quickly *access important information* such as the app version, notification, and alert settings with fewer clicks, 
so that I can have a more efficient and consistent experience across devices.

h2. Acceptance Criteria:
 - The app version must be easily accessible within the app settings.
 - Users must be able to enable/disable notifications and alerts within [specific section, e.g., settings or a dedicated menu.
 - The navigation flow for accessing these settings must require fewer clicks compared to the previous version.
 - The user experience for accessing this information should be consistent across both desktop and mobile applications.

h3. [UX on Figma|https://www.figma.com/design/IgfSERyqCoRH3Iyejre5oE/Lens-Mobile?node-id=51462-4355&t=WRRxChnnkYPQL5vd-1]

!image-2025-03-05-17-39-49-734.png|width=60%!


h1. ---- ORGINAL DESCRIPTION

As an end-user of the Lens Mobile app, I'm seeking a way to quickly access important information within the app with fewer clicks, such as the app version, enable/disable notifications and alerts.
I would also like to have a consistent user experience across both desktop and mobile applications.

*Given* that the end-user has the Lens mobile app installed AND
*When* searching for quickly accessible information such as app version, notifications, and alert settings,
*Then* this it is easily accessible in the app with fewer clicks.  

 

*Figma:* LMW-121

column830:
[~krzysztof.wiecek@hp.com] Engineering recommend going forward with this release based on the following pragmatics

LMW-295 100% Reproduced & Resolved

LMW-294 Background Crash - 80% certainty resolved - The user doesn't experience that crash, it happens when the app is in the background

The recent adoption of Swift 6 has introduced instabilities that went undetected during QA. The reported crashes have put us in a difficult position, as we are unable to reproduce them. These crashes occur when the app is running in the background.
 * Due to the introduction of Swift 6 on 4.5.1 release
 * Reported to [Firebase|https://console.firebase.google.com/u/0/project/plantronics-backbeat/crashlytics/app/ios:com.plantronics.myheadset/issues?state=open&time=last-seven-days&types=crash&tag=all&sort=eventCount] a few hours after the 4.5.1 release
 * {*}The Crash fix is READY{*}, but there is no way knowing if this is a "complete" fix since the crash doesn't occur on our side.
 * {*}Detail{*}: The adoption of Swift 6 introduced iOS app instabilities due to concurrency control changes. The crash, which occurs after several hours of idling, is challenging to reproduce—only Krzysztof (manual QA) managed to trigger it once. A potential fix addressing a {{MainActor}} warning has been implemented, with an 80% certainty it resolves the issue. The problem stems from missed cases where functions weren't consistently marked as {{{}MainActor{}}}, a gap from the initial migration by Eugene (ex contractor).
 * VALIDATION: No way to 100% validate the fix, but VERY high confidence it will.


!screenshot-1.png|width=40%!

Flamingo and Spoonbill support OTA DFU for the Lagoon charging case, and the device names are visible at the top of the supported devices list with a "New" tag.

column1: iOS | CANCELLED | DFU | LMW-26 | New dialog copies for connection issues/device low battery scenarios for DFU
column34:
*WHO*

iOS dev

*WHAT*

A new dialog is needed for connection problems with charge case when user taps for update device.

*WHY*

As a user I would like to be informed in a proper way why my device can't start a firmware update.

Right now user will get a dialog message that a device is low battery even when some connection issues occur. For example, if a Voyager Legend 50 gets an update, but for some reason there is a connection problem with a charge case, and user taps update all button, a charge case low battery dialog will be displayed. This may lead to confusion, especially when user connects charge case to power and will receive the same alert.

Implementing a proper dialog for connection problems, or some generic dialog other than low battery will allow a user to take proper steps to eliminate the problem and start DFU.

Right now, we are showing those dialogs for device problems before DFU starts:
||Device ||DFU Scenario||Low Battery of||New Recommendations (if applies)||
|Voyager Free 60+ UC (OSPREY
AND FLAMINGO)|Charge Case FW update is available|Charge Case |{color:#00875a}*Device charging case battery is too low to update the software. Connect your device charging case to a power source and try again.*{color}|
| |ONLY the Earbuds FW update is available|Earbuds|{color:#00875a}*Be sure your earbuds are out of the case, connected, and fully charged to complete the update.*{color}|
| |BOTH, the Charge Case and Earbuds (or headset) FW update are available|Charge Case and Earbuds/Headset|{color:#00875a}*Not enough battery remaining to update the software. Connect your device and charging case to a power source and try again.*{color}|
|Voyager Free 20/60 (EMU/TERN)|ONLY earbuds FW update is possible in the MOBILE app|Earbuds|{color:#00875a}*Be sure your earbuds are out of the case, connected, and fully charged to complete the update.*{color}|
|All other headsets ONLY|ONLY Headset
(NO charge case, NO charge stand)|HEADSET|{color:#00875a}*Device battery is too low to update the software. Connect your device to a power source and try again.*{color}|

 

*We don't cover any charge case disconnected scenarios, example:*

If user will leave Voyager Legend 50 charge case at home, and will try to perform DFU, low battery dialog will be shown or, the update button for charge case will remain unresponsive.

column1: ANDROID | Remove dev cloud environment from build process
column34:
*Who*

Android dev team

*What*

Remove dev cloud environment from codebase. Only leave the enums so we don't lose it completely.

*Why*

Cloud dev is very unstable, and we have code generation based on online schema. This means that if they make an incompatible change (eg. remove a field) then our code won't compile - this blocks every dev's work in various ways

*Acceptance criteria*
 * project build no longer relies on cloud dev environment
 * cloud dev environment enum value is still in codebase

column1: ANDROID | DSDK | O4 | Clean up DevicesListProvider - make DeviceInfo non-mutable
column34:
*NOTE: this will be one of largest tasks, but it's hard to split it into smaller parts. However if possible - split it into multiple PRs*

*Who*
Android dev team

*What*
 * make all DeviceInfo's fields immutable constructor params
 * transform device cache (deviceListSubject) into a StateFlow
 * transform updateDeviceInfo so it only replaces the old DeviceInfo with new one by device id
 * save devices to store on each update by observing the StateFlow
 * remember that StateFlow has it's internal diffing mechanism so once DeviceInfo is immutable data class there's no need to explicitly state if something was changed or not (no need for boolean result in updateDeviceInfo's lambda argument's return)
 * simplify or get rid of updateDeviceInfo - depends on how you approach changing device's state
 * make sure that DeviceInfo's toString method is not used for some external tracking or other app features - and then remove the override

*Why*
Refer to the epic

 

*Acceptance Criteria*
 * DeviceInfo is a data class with immutable properties and contains derived properties as needed

column1: ANDROID | DSDK | O4 | Clean up DevicesListProvider - analytics part
column34:
*Who*
Android dev team

*What*
 * remove whole analytics tracking from DevicesListProvider. Each call to analytics (tracking call) is called upon device state change or other distinct event (like first devices load) - this can be done in a separate component that would just observe the devices from DevicesListProvider's devices observable
 * this applies to sendDeviceInfoState and sendDeviceInfoUpdate. If you find any additional tracking calls, include those in the change as well
 * in other task about hubnative.DeviceManager analytics - there is already a proposed component - CloudAnalytics, we should probably add the code there

*Why*
Refer to the epic

 

*Acceptance Criteria*
 * DevicesListProvider doesn't call Cloud or IoT tracking at all
 * Tracking calls are called by observing data from DevicesListProvider 
 * Tracking is done in app's coroutine scope or it's own (singleton) scope

column1: ANDROID | DSDK | O1 | Clean up relation between Accessory-Device-DeviceInfo
column34:
*NOTE: it will be one of hardest tasks to do right*

Device is an util class for CommunicatorImpl, but if you look closer, it's quite redundant and cofusing

*Who*
Android dev team

*What*
 * Device.setBlockSizeForFtp is something that should be done in DevicesListProviderImpl
 * Device.messageOutput and connections are already located in Accessory and copied into Device - so just maintain them inside Accessory directly
 * reduce data held in Accessory from DeviceInfo to only really used data - id, supportedCommands, isPaired, lensAddress. Keep lendsAddress split from id - as opposed to keeping these two in DeviceId
 * Create DeviceInfo once in DevicesListProvider for each accessory

*Why*
Refer to the epic

 

*Acceptance Criteria*
 * Device class is removed
 * Accessory doesn't keep full DeviceInfo inside
 * DeviceInfo has 1-1 relation with Accessory and is created only in DevicesListProvider 
 * Device management and DFU work as it did before this refactor

Flamingo and Spoonbill support OTA DFU for the Lagoon charging case{-}, and the device names should be visible at the top of the supported devices list as a new launch.{-}

column1: iOS  Implement a new dialog when iPhone battery is low
column34:
Before performing DFU, the Lens app should check iPhone battery status to ensure there’s enough charge or that the device is connected to power. This prevents unexpected shutdowns during the update, which could lead to potential issues and ensure a smooth, successful process.

*Acceptance criteria:*

*Given* the Lens app's user is about to perform a DFU,  
*When* iPhone battery status is below *30%* and is *not* connected to power,  
*Then* the dialog should be presented with message "{_}Your device battery doesn't have enough power to update the software. Fully charge the device or plug it into a power source and try again{_}."

*NOTE:*

The acceptance criteria are taken from Android implementation.

column1: UX | Poly Lens Menu
column34:
As a user of the Lens Mobile app, I'm seeking a way to quickly access important information within the app with fewer clicks.

*Problem Statement:*
As an example, currently, it takes me 3 clicks just to view the build number of the app I have installed. 
In a recent discussion with [~vann.geondeff@hp.com], he mentioned that the step for turning off the alert in the app could be more user-friendly and intuitive. Any alter enable/disable is 2 levels down in the app.
 
*Product Ask:*
 
Attached is a screenshot of the [Poly Lens Menu -> General|https://www.figma.com/design/IgfSERyqCoRH3Iyejre5oE/Lens-Mobile?node-id=17096-132894&node-type=frame&t=tckVv8erFex4pTcp-0], which shows the application settings available to the end user. 
These settings might enhance the app's user experience if placed differently.
 
*Figma:* [https://www.figma.com/design/IgfSERyqCoRH3Iyejre5oE/Lens-Mobile?node-id=51462-4355&t=WRRxChnnkYPQL5vd-1]
column830:
Based on the Figma update, the color theme has now been moved under General, as requested in the previous comment. 

The following request is pending but will hopefully be addressed within the scope of the Poly Studio effort.
{color:#de350b}- Additionally, a Notifications & Alerts menu could use the similar bell icon !image-2025-01-27-18-36-49-145.png|width=26,height=31! from Studio experience, instead of existing !image-2025-01-27-18-37-24-703.png|width=20,height=20! icon.{color}


The other changes discussed for Poly Lens menu restructure discussed in this ticket are complete in Figma, and now marking the following implementation stories as ready for refinement.
LMW-412 iOS | DRAFT | Restructure Poly Lens Menu
LMW-413 ANDROID | DRAFT | Restructure Poly Lens Menu
 
CC: [~alexandra.casique@hp.com], [~ujjaval.gupta@hp.com]
column1623:
26/Nov/24 11:30 AM;alexandra.casique@hp.com;Hi, [~seema.nikam@hp.com] 
We discussed the need for categorizing elements, so some items will naturally fall under specific categories. Displaying every item outside of these categories would make features harder to locate. A few clicks—two or three—to access a feature is standard practice and ensures better organization and usability.

For the build number, we suggest this change [Figma|https://www.figma.com/design/IgfSERyqCoRH3Iyejre5oE/Lens-Mobile?node-id=50892-8781&t=ubjO2XaH7FqrwcfE-1]
However, we should keep the 'About' with the Release Notes. 

cc: [~kem.gallione@hp.com] ;;;
column1624:
9/Dec/24 5:33 PM;seema.nikam@hp.com;Hi [~alexandra.casique@hp.com] 
Should we consider placing the About tab under the Poly Lens Menu to align with the Lens Desktop UX?

Moreover, relocating the Privacy Policy tab as a link under a new About tab within the Poly Lens Menu, similar to the Lens Desktop setup, would provide a consistent user experience across all Lens applications.


!image-2024-12-09-11-33-04-210.png|width=660,height=464!

column1626:
22/Jan/25 4:56 PM;seema.nikam@hp.com;[~alexandra.casique@hp.com] Could you provide the UX perspective for the following?

1.  The Notifications & Alerts menu in Lens Mobile, as outlined in the problem description?

It is available under the Poly Lens Menu -> General tab for the Lens Mobile today. However, this menu is available in the Poly Lens Menu on the Lens Desktop. 

If the space limitation is the concern, then I understand to keep an existing experience.

2. I also noticed that starting with the LD-2.0 release, certain notifications (Device charging, Mute alert) are disabled by default (on app install/update).
Was this a UX decision to disable high-frequency alerts?

If so, I would like to propose we have the same experience for these 2 alerts to be disabled by default on Lens Mobile as well. 

Please let me know your thoughts.;;;

column1: ANDROID | FALCON | BDS | Charge stand and headset DFU (Over-The-Air One-Step DFU)
column34:
*Pre-requisite:*

1. The firmware team would need to provide support with deckards that would enable us to provide basic device support for the charge stand.{*}{{*}}

2. As this feature is supported for other similar devices (charge cases for Voyager Free 60+ UC, Voyager Legend 50 and 30) in the Lens Mobile, the headset team will validate this feature through self-serve effort in the scope of LENS-23122 and report any issues identified. **

 

*Who, what, why?*
As an end-user of the Falcon headset model, I would like the Lens Mobile app to update the firmware on the charge stand when the headset is docked (via OTA DFU).
 
*Acceptance criteria:*
*Given* the Falcon headset user docks the headset on the Falcon charge stand
*When* the user initiates the Firmware update for the headset
*Then,* the headset also updates the firmware of the charge stand via OTA DFU feature, if the update is available for the charge stand

column1: ANDROID | UX | CANCELED | Notifications
column34:
*Who, what, why:*

As a UX designer, my objective is to continually update the app's UI and improve user experience. Similarly, as a product manager, my goal is to ensure the app fulfills user expectations and remains competitive in the market.

The UX team has designs for a new and improved user experience design. This effort will progress through phases to align with our other Lens client offerings, like Desktop app 2.0 (along with broader Poly Studio). Ultimately, the goal is to provide a seamless and consistent user experience across all Lens platforms.

 

*Pre-requisite:* Phase 1

 

*Figma:* [Future Lens Mobile|https://www.figma.com/design/IgfSERyqCoRH3Iyejre5oE/Lens-Mobile?node-id=37690-28359&t=4SsPv4YUcHEQTnxv-0]

 

*Acceptance Criteria:* Improved end-user experience for the notifications in the app and the Hybrid DFU initiation workflow (TBD).
Please refer to the individual stories in this epic.

 

*Notes:*
 # The improved user experience design aims to solve feature requests by our testers during user testing. For example, updating the notification frequency etc: LENS-9284

column1: ANDROID | FALCON 🎧 NF | Display charging stand's charging status
column34:
h3. User Story

As a user, I want to check whether my headset is charging from the Lens Mobile app when it placed on the charge stand. (!)

As a developer, I want to provide the charging status for the charge stand as requested by the spec. 
h3. Acceptance criteria
 # *Given* the Falcon headset is paired with my mobile
 ## *And* the Falcon has a charge stand connected to a power supply
 # *When* the user puts headset on the charge stand for charging
 # *Then* the Lens Mobile App will reflect the charging state in the status box

h3. Pre-requisites

1. The firmware team to provide support with deckards to read charging status from the charge stand.
2. [Latest Figma link (As of 14/04/25)|https://www.figma.com/design/kVruq94TvQDlPCELGmJcs5/Studio-Mobile-Spec?node-id=506-2697&t=GDzZUKNsU519Q7tu-1]
h3. Questions
 - Should the app reflect whether it is charging another device than the headset?
column830:

I am not sure if Lens UI needs to show how the headset is charging i.e. via hardwired USB or by docking on the charge stand. Just showing the charging on the battery icon is good enough. 
I think it would be good for Lens UI to show a different image when the headset is paired with a charge stand. Taking it further if it is paired show UI docked v/s undocked.

column1: iOS  Retrofit battery levels icons 🔋 🪫 to match battery STATUS from device (HS or CC)
column34:
Currently the app queries the battery level as an integer from 0 to 100. The received level is then mapped to an icon and color scheme.

The firmware also implements a {*}battery status{*}: {{{}Full{}}}{*},{*} {{{}High{}}}{*},{*} {{{}Medium{}}}{*},{*} {{{}Low{}}}{*},{*} {{{}Critical{}}}{*}, and{*} {{{}Flat{}}}.

Simplify the logic by mapping icons and colors directly to the battery status instead of using the percentage. We still show the % next to the icon.

LMW-457 iOS | Retrofit battery levels icons to match battery STATUS from device (HS or CC)
LMW-489  ANDROID | Retrofit battery levels icons to match battery STATUS from device (HS or CC)

Lens Clients must use the Deckard Device Battery Level Setting and Event {{0x0A2A}} to gather and receive the *level* (battery percentage) and *chargeLevel* (critical, low, medium, high, etc). This setting/event includes the device type ({*}charger case{*}, {*}left{*}, {*}right{*}, or {*}headset{*}) so that Lens can get the battery data for each device in the connected device set. For consistency, this message should also be used for headsets, where the device type will be "headset".

[Lens Mobile UI Library Figma Spec |https://www.figma.com/design/bTBoWeajYswVFRQwinCW8e/Lens-Mobile-UI-Library?node-id=4047-961&t=Ij8Cpi5N9say1WYQ-0]

column1: ANDROID | LMW-26 | Display generic dialog for DFU failure when its not a low-battery scenario
column34:
h1. AFTER {color:#de350b}iOS{color} ENGINEERING REVIEW - LMW-27 
h2. *Scenario A1* - {{otaDfuReady}} returns true
 # *Given* Lens device is connected to Lens app
 # *When* user initiates DFU for connected device
*And* {{otaDfuReady}} returns true
 # *Then* DFU is initiated and finished with success

h2. *Scenario A2* - {{otaDfuReady}} returns true (alternative)
 # *Given* Lens device is connected to Lens app
 # *When* user initiates DFU for connected device
*And* {{otaDfuReady}} returns true
 # *Then* DFU is initiated
 # *When* DFU fails
 # *Then* Houston (error) screen is displayed
*And* user can select "Cancel" or "Retry"

h2. *Scenario B1* - {{otaDfuReady}} returns false
 # *Given* Lens device is connected to Lens app
*And* Lens device is fully charge
 # *When* user initiates DFU for connected device
*And* otaDfuReady return false
 # *Then* inform user with the generic dialog "Update currently not available, please try back later."

h2. *Scenario B2* - {{otaDfuReady}} returns false
 # *Given* Lens device is connected to Lens app
*And* the {color:#00875a}supported device battery is low{color} (FW determines the threshold level)
 # *When* user initiates DFU for connected device
*And* {{otaDfuReady}} return false
 # *Then* inform user with the generic dialog "Update currently not available, please try back later."

h1.  
h1. ORIGINAL

Refer LMW-26 for {*}Figma{*}.

*UPDATE: 01/27*

*Given* the user initiates the DFU for a connected BT device, AND 
the device is fully charged with the otaDFUReady flag reported as Yes, but
*When* the DFU process still cannot be initiated for an unknown reason, 
*Then* inform the user with the generic dialog stating "Update currently not available, please try back later.", instead of displaying the misleading low battery dialog.

 

Please refer to the attached screenshot-2.png (a comment from UX ticket: LMW-26), for more details on existing vs. new dialog text copy.

column1623:
18/Nov/24 1:35 PM;michal.szatkowski@hp.com;[~seema.nikam@hp.com], as [~miljan.vukicevic@hp.com]  mentioned in the [LMW-26|https://hp-jira.external.hp.com/browse/LMW-26] comment, the app cannot determine the exact reason why the DFU cannot be performed. Therefore, it cannot differentiate the dialog between battery-related and non-battery-related issues.

This means there can only be a single dialog to cover all possible reasons.

In this case, we have two options:
a. Stay with the current dialog, which assumes the issue is due to low battery.
b. Update the dialog message to a more generic one, such as "Update currently not available, please try again later," as proposed by Nikita in [LMW-26|https://hp-jira.external.hp.com/browse/LMW-26].;;;

column1: ANDROID | LENS-25164 | POLICY | Adding additional support for ANC/Transparency on cloud and Android app
column34:
{*}WHO{*}:
Android development team with support from the cloud team

{*}WHAT{*}:
To ensure ANC/Transparency features work correctly with cloud integration, we need to add an additional cloud ID to the Android codebase, linked to the setting/event associated with Deckard ID: {{0x0430}} (global setting ID: {{{}0x1503{}}}). While Android already supports {{{}0x1503{}}}, the cloud team needs to customize the values being sent. The new cloud ID format should be confirmed with Igor, and the current Android implementation will need to be modified accordingly.

{*}WHY{*}:
As a user of the Android app connected to a cloud tenant, I want my policies to function properly in accordance with ANC/Transparency settings for my headset. This is currently not possible without collaboration between the Android and cloud teams to ensure proper handling of cloud IDs.

{*}ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA{*}:
 # The cloud ID for {{0x0430}} is successfully implemented in the Android app.
 # The Android app is tested to confirm that policies work as expected when connected to a cloud tenant.
 # The cloud team provides the required cloud ID format, and the Android implementation is updated and tested accordingly.

column1: ANDROID 🟥 FALCON 🎧 Implement Onboarding Guide
column34:
*Update - 3/2/26*
As a first time user of the new Poly Focus headset, I'd like the Studio app to help me familiarize with the new headset & show me how I can customize my experience to my preferences so that I can get the most out of my Poly Focus headset.

[Figma |https://www.figma.com/design/kVruq94TvQDlPCELGmJcs5/Studio-Mobile-Spec?node-id=6486-14100&t=rp8aHAeGJKXT65HP-4]

*Acceptance Criteria*
 * The trigger criteria for onboarding guide will remain the same as for other headsets.

*Out of Scope*
 * Renaming the headset during onboarding.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

As a user of a new HP/Poly BT headset, I want an out-of-the-box experience (Basic Device Support) for an onboarding guide similar to the support for existing supported devices in the app.  

*Pre-requisite:* UX in Figma: LENS-24033
h2. Acceptance criteria:

*Given* your HP/poly, the new BT Falcon headset model is paired with your smartphone, 
*When* the user opens the Lens mobile app using the Quick QR code
*Then* the Lens app offers the OOBE (Out-Of-the-Box-Experience) for easy setup and a walk-through of Falcon's main features and controls AND
The app displays key controls and differentiating features during the first setup of the Lens mobile app. 
-Few examples:-
 # -Pairing methods (currently exist for Osprey/Tern/Emu)-
 # -EQ (currently exist for Osprey/Tern/Emu)-
 # -Wearing style - replaceable earcups- on-the-ear and over-the-ear-
 # -Replaceable battery-
 # -New Settings as differentiators from Falcon's predecessors-
 ## -[DND (General)|https://rndwiki.inc.hpicorp.net/confluence/display/HBU/General]-
 ## -[NoiseBlock AI feature/CCL|https://rndwiki.inc.hpicorp.net/confluence/display/HBU/Advanced]-

column34:
*Who, What & Why:*

UX decided to redesign the EQ configuration selector.

*Acceptance criteria:*
 * A seperate screen where user can select between presets and custom EQ configuration opens when user clicks the button under the "Equalizer" label.
 * Selected option has a check icon to the right and always reflects the currently selected configuration.
 * The new screen looks as per Figma
 * The selector button looks as per Figma (only displays the current config name)
 * When user selects the configuration it is applied to the device and:
 ** The selection screen closes
 ** Proper EQ screen displayed so either the readonly screen for presets or the editable screen for Custom configuration
 ** The selector button displays the name of the selected configuration
 ** Levels adjust to the values of the current configuration

column1: iOS | Adding support for "Voice & Prompts / Calls" settings for Chronos devices.
column34:
{*}WHO{*}:

iOS dev team

 

{*}WHAT{*}:

Implementation on missing setting in *Voice & Prompts / Calls* global setting id 0x1301.

According to UI spec this setting is responsible for controlling additional prompts: 

*Incoming Call 0x1302*

*Answering Call 0x0601*

*Answer/Ignore 0x1303*

*Active Audio Tone 0x060C*

 

Screenshot from UI Spec attached.

 

{*}WHY{*}:

As an app user I should have access to all settings included in UI spec. This behavior is already implemented in Lens Desktop.

 

*Acceptance Criteria:*

Voice & Prompts / Calls is implemented and working as expected.

Turning off/on "Calls" will trigger hiding related settings.

For reference feature is already implemented in Desktop apps.

column1: iOS | LENS-25164 | POLICY | Adding additional support for ANC/Transparency on cloud and iOS app with cloud id supporting deckard id: 0x0430
column34:
WHO: 

iOS dev team with cloud support

WHAT:

ANC/Transparency to working correctly on cloud requires adding additional cloud id to our codebase connected to setting/event with deckard id: 0x0430 (global setting id 0x1503).

WHY:

As iOS app user connected to cloud tenant I would like to get my policies working correctly according to ANC/Transparency settings for my headsets. It is not possible at the moment without additional action from iOS and cloud teams.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:

Successfully implementing cloud id for 0x0430 in iOS app and test it on cloud when implemented there.

Required cloud id have to be provided by cloud team.

column1625:
16/Aug/24 11:42 AM;sebastian.hat-poly@hp.com;Today we had a meeting with [~piotr.jamroz@hp.com], [~igor.despot@hp.com], and myself, where Piotr was explaining how new implementation of ANC/Transparency is working from iOS and device perspective.

Three deckard ids are working together and communicating with a hardware element to ensure correct set for ANC/Transparency. They all need to be set together, but with some delay, because hardware element cannot handle fast changes of all three at once.;;;
