column1: ANDROID | FALCON | NF | Busy Light | Support new setting for DND (Do Not Disturb)
column34:
h2. [User Story - Falcon Headset – Lens App Integration for DND|https://github.azc.ext.hp.com/Poly-Lens-Apps/Lens-App-iOS/wiki/GWT-%E2%80%90-Do-Not-Disturb-setting]

As a {*}Falcon headset user{*}, I want to communicate my availability to others via the Dot Not Disturb headset LED preventing interruptions
h2. Rule-based Acceptance
 - *manually toggle* the busy light via Lens to control my {*}DND status{*}.

h2. Scenario-oriented Acceptance Criteria
 # *Given* the headset supports the DND setting
 ** *And* the DND is initially {{Off}}
 # *When* the user turns On the “Do Not Disturb” setting (switch)
 # *Then* the “busy light” turns {{On}} on the headset (blinks/flashs)
 # *When* the user turns Off the “Do Not Disturb” setting (switch)
 # *Then* the “busy light” turns {{Off}} on the headset and stops flashing


h1. — ORIGINAL —

As a Falcon headset user, I'd like the Lens app to integrate the Do Not Disturb feature from my headset. This way, my DND (Do Not Disturb) status can be visible to others.
 

*Who/What/Why?*
As a Falcon headset user,
I want my busy light to turn on (red) when I am on a mobile or UC call and turn off when I am not on a call AND
I want to be able to switch the busy light on and off via Lens to allow me to enter or leave the DND state AND
I want my DND state to override the call state for Busy Light, 
so that my on-call and busy status is visible to others to avoid interruptions.

 

*Pre-requisite:*

1. Firmware will support Do Not Disturb "On" or Off" as a new Falcon setting, based on the Source of truth from [Global UI Spec -> General -> Do Not Disturb|https://rndwiki.inc.hpicorp.net/confluence/display/HBU/General] setting.

2. Figma UI spec indicating how to represent this new setting in the Lens mobile app. Please refer to LENS-24034.

3. [Falcon UI Spec|https://hp.sharepoint.com/teams/UISpecifications-Falcon/Shared%20Documents/Forms/AllItems.aspx?FolderCTID=0x01200022FE5FCF4FE3F940B909321DD3E185C3&id=%2Fteams%2FUISpecifications%2DFalcon%2FShared%20Documents%2FUI%20Specification] provides the setting details. 
Following the latest [UI Specification update on August 30th|https://hp.sharepoint.com/teams/UISpecifications-Falcon/Shared%20Documents/Forms/AllItems.aspx?id=%2Fteams%2FUISpecifications%2DFalcon%2FShared%20Documents%2FUI%20Specification&sortField=Modified&isAscending=false&viewid=e64641e0%2D6ad4%2D4d08%2Da90f%2D85e8607b6823], a new feature called "Online Indicator" (which only relates to the active call indication) has been introduced under the General settings category.

 

*Acceptance criteria:*

*Given* the Falcon headset user can change the DND (Do Not Disturb) setting
*When* the user changes the DND setting to turn ON
*Then,* the busy light on the headset turns ON (red) 
and the user can switch the busy light OFF via the Lens app to leave the DND (Do Not Disturb) state

*Given* the Falcon headset user can change the DND (Do Not Disturb) setting
*When* the user changes the DND setting to turn OFF
*Then,* the busy light on the headset turns OFF
and the user can switch the busy light to ON via the Lens app to enter the DND (Do Not Disturb) state again

 

*FAQs:*

1) During the call the setting DND will be changed, will it be done automatically by FW?

If the user on Lens mobile enabled DND, FW should not change the setting until the user turns DND off

2) Light is a physical indicator on Falcon Accessory?

The busy light is on the headset.

3) Could you please clarify, what it mean: "{*}The user wants the busy light to turn on (red){*}
*and to be able to switch the busy light on and off via the Lens app to enter or leave the DND (Do Not Disturb) state*
{*}and the DND state to override the call state for Busy Light{*}"

There are 2 ways of showing Busy light on the headset 

a) During a call 

b) When the user sets DND on the Lens Mobile app 

If the user sets DND ON, then it takes priority over "a)".

column1: ANDROID | FALCON | Post-FA | Rename device during initial setup (Onboarding)
column34:
As an end-user, I should be able to name my device during initial setup, for example, "Johns Poly Falcon 1".

h2. Acceptance criteria

h3. Rule-based acceptance criteria
- The user will be able to change the device's name during Onboarding (triggered on initial setup)
- The app should display the new device name in the settings screen and on the device list
- The new device name should persist across app sessions, even after the app is closed and reopened.
- The Rename feature is always available from Onboarding, not only on initial setup


h3. Main Scenario: Rename device during onboarding

h4. Rename during Initial Setup (Onboarding)
 # *Given* the Falcon headset has never been setup with the Lens Mobile app
 # *When* the user pairs the headset via Bluetooth
 # *Then* the mobile asks to open the Lens Mobile app
 # *When* the user accepts to open the Lens Mobile app
 # *Then* the Lens Mobile initiates the Falcon headset Onboarding (initial setup)
 # *When* the user proceeds with Onboarding
 # *Then* the Lens Mobile app prompts to rename the device
 # *When* the user enters a new name
 ** *And* presses {{Next}} or {{Save}}
 # *Then* the Lens Mobile app saves the device name (deckard command)
 ** *And* shows the new name on the screen
 # *When* the user disconnects the headset from Bluetooth
 # *Then* the headset shows disconnected on the home screen
 # *When* the user restarts the Lens Mobile app
 ** *And* reconnects the headset to Bluetooth
 # *Then* the new headset name was persisted as set during onboarding
 ** *And* shows connected

h4. In Short

# Given Falcon onboarding is launched
# When the user clicks Rename on Rename Onboarding page
# Then app shows a rename screen
# When user inputs a name into the field 
   ## And clicks save
# Then Falcon saves the name input
    ## And the name is persisted on the headset
    ## And the name on the onboarding page is also changed

h2. Known limitation

There are known OS-specific limitations on how and when a new name gets reflected in the Bluetooth settings, the UX team’s input and an update to the existing user experience are critical.
For example: a warning or notification for an end-user to inform.

h2. Prerequisites

Osprey example
[Rename Figma|https://www.figma.com/design/IgfSERyqCoRH3Iyejre5oE/Lens-Mobile?node-id=50063-81107&t=NbbWHNYvV1l0XMi0-1]

Falcon (FPO)
[Rename Falcon FPO|https://www.figma.com/design/IgfSERyqCoRH3Iyejre5oE/Lens-Mobile?node-id=50065-82512&t=NbbWHNYvV1l0XMi0-1]

- The user will be able to change the device's name during Onboarding (triggered on initial setup)
- The app should display the new device name in the settings screen and on the device list
- The new device name should persist across app sessions, even after the app is closed and reopened.
- The Rename feature is always available from Onboarding, not only on initial setup


h3. Main Scenario: Rename device during onboarding

h4. Rename during Initial Setup (Onboarding)
 # *Given* the Falcon headset has never been setup with the Lens Mobile app
 # *When* the user pairs the headset via Bluetooth
 # *Then* the mobile asks to open the Lens Mobile app
 # *When* the user accepts to open the Lens Mobile app
 # *Then* the Lens Mobile initiates the Falcon headset Onboarding (initial setup)
 # *When* the user proceeds with Onboarding
 # *Then* the Lens Mobile app prompts to rename the device
 # *When* the user enters a new name
 ** *And* presses {{Next}} or {{Save}}
 # *Then* the Lens Mobile app saves the device name (deckard command)
 ** *And* shows the new name on the screen
 # *When* the user disconnects the headset from Bluetooth
 # *Then* the headset shows disconnected on the home screen
 # *When* the user restarts the Lens Mobile app
 ** *And* reconnects the headset to Bluetooth
 # *Then* the new headset name was persisted as set during onboarding
 ** *And* shows connected

h4. In Short

# Given Falcon onboarding is launched
# When the user clicks Rename on Rename Onboarding page
# Then app shows a rename screen
# When user inputs a name into the field 
   ## And clicks save
# Then Falcon saves the name input
    ## And the name is persisted on the headset
    ## And the name on the onboarding page is also changed

h2. Known limitation

There are known OS-specific limitations on how and when a new name gets reflected in the Bluetooth settings, the UX team’s input and an update to the existing user experience are critical.
For example: a warning or notification for an end-user to inform.

h2. Prerequisites

Osprey example
[Rename Figma|https://www.figma.com/design/IgfSERyqCoRH3Iyejre5oE/Lens-Mobile?node-id=50063-81107&t=NbbWHNYvV1l0XMi0-1]

Falcon (FPO)
[Rename Falcon FPO|https://www.figma.com/design/IgfSERyqCoRH3Iyejre5oE/Lens-Mobile?node-id=50065-82512&t=NbbWHNYvV1l0XMi0-1]

column1623:
8/May/24 7:20 PM;kem.gallione@hp.com;[~seema.nikam@hp.com] This is being looked at kind of the opposite way that it's intended. As we talked about before we are only showing that multiple devices for Osprey need updates because updating that particular device and it's case together can take almost 18 minutes to update so we are allowing the user to only update one at a time or update all in that particular situation. Thats why the new screen was created. We also use that new screen to let the user know that they have multiple devices needing updates for that product but we only support updating one (the earbuds, and not the case) and that they need to update the case through Lens Desktop. Those particular screens are kind of band aid screens that we had to implement because of limitations that don't really allow for an "update all" condition. The main goal has been to update everything without the user needing to know and keep track of everything being updated. The experience is to simply have the one click update and everything just updates and works. That new screen was created for conditions where thats not either possible or in Ospreys case, not ideal. 

I went over this particular topic with multiple people on our UX team and they have also confirmed that this is indeed the desired DFU path. ;;;

column1625:
21/Jun/24 4:14 PM;joel.maranhao@hp.com;[~seema.nikam@hp.com] 
It is difficult to work with a list of notes and feedback. 
Engineering needs a clear scenario (*Give* [preconditions], *When* [action], *Then* [expected result])

h4. My recommendations to author your tickets:
- The *title* of the ticket should be goal oriented or should describe the use case _e.g. Improve OTA DFU to show the Poly accessory's name being updated_
- The *description* should have a user story (who, what and why) and a set of acceptance criteria (rule-based or scenario-oriented).
Note that the user story statement is different from a list of acceptance criteria.

example of a scenario oriented acceptance criteria

- 1. *Given* your Poly Accessory is paired with your phone
    *And* the Lens App shows the accessory is {{connected}}
    *And* the Lens App shows that firmware updates are available
- 2. *When* the user clicks the {{Update}} button
- 3. *Then* the Lens App ... _describe here the expected result_



See [~michal.szatkowski@hp.com] note too

CC [~wendy.dawson@hp.com] [~forrest.hardy@hp.com]

column1: ANDROID | CANCELED | DISCOVERY | HP Docks onboarding experience over WiFi, through Lens Mobile (SPIKE)
column34:
*Updated story by Engineering:*

*Who, what, why:*

As a home worker, when I receive an HP dock from my employer I would like a simple method [using the Lens mobile app] to configure the HP dock to connect to my home wireless network so that it can connect to the internet, it can register with and be provisioned by Poly Lens Cloud and it can download firmware updates.  I would like this functionality as it is more convenient and requires less technical skills than logging into the WebUI of the HP dock station to manually configure this.

*Acceptance Criteria:*
 * The initial deliverable is to work with Dragan to formulate an agreed upon sequence diagram which will outline the anticipated flow.  This will enable the dev team to consider technical solutions better.

 

 

*Notes from PO:*

This is the discovery work in discussion and there is no commitment assigned to it. 
 
As an end-user of the HP dock, I want to onboard it to the Lens tenant easily. 
 
Proposed user workflow:
 # The HP Dock broadcasts a fixed, known, WiFi SSID
 # Lens Mobile app starts
 # Goes it as a new "HP Dock" mode
 # Finds fixed SSID and connects
 # Lens Mobile acts as a proxy to download the latest FW to dock
 # Dock updates to the latest FW
 # <nothing else, no lens log-in, no lens provisioning>

 
Known facts:
 # Docks don't have Bladerunner spec implemented so we need to decide if that is needed or if we can do something simpler, more targeted.
 # The docks are in an alpha state - just raw boards and the process is manual to update.  So, the developer can understand what you are getting into.
 # Lens Product leadership proposed we should use LDT / Loginless vs mobile, but the timeline with LDT seems promising.

column1: ANDROID | Refactor LensNotificationManager and LensNotification
column34:
*Who*
Android Development Team

*What*
LensNotificationManager should take care of all communication with the system, possibly with injected factory of notifications (mapping from LensNotification to android system notification). LensNotification should be a pure data class

*Why*
Right now LensNotification is a weird mix of data class + factory + command patterns, basically it's responsible for what LensNotificationManager should be. It's causing weird practices, like passing context around to (this) data class, checking permissions and accessing android system - everything that should be done by LensNotificationManager.

column1: ANDROID | DISCOVERY | Possible improvements to notifications
column34:
Things to discuss when time allows for it:
 * {*}don't cancel all notifications on being to foreground{*}- I don't think we should cancel all notifications on bringing app to foreground, since some of them show even when app is foreground. We should cancel only those notifs, that are background only.
 * *split earbuds battery notifications* - we don't show low battery if state is already charging. For earbuds we're not tracking individual earbuds, rather we're merging 3 states into 1, so eg. if 1 element battery is charging (eg. left earbud), and other is low - then we won't show 'low battery' notification, because merged state already is in 'is charging' state.
 * *reconsider show conditions for update available notification* - this notifications is only showing 'once per session' - depending on the device and user behavior it can be once every hour, or once every week or even longer. Also it shows in background only. So user has to launch the app then move it to background, and then we have to hope that some update to device states happen. I suggest we just show this notification once every X days, like once every week (per device). And not tie it to user sessions, so we can show it from background work even if user didn't touch the app for 2 weeks.
 * *remove redundant, confusing notifications channel* - we have a notification channel named 'Unknown' - that's visible to users in system app settings. I don't think it's used, so I don't see a reason to keep it. On other hand we could add 'General' notifications channel
 * *improve hubNative initialization* - HubNative along with DeviceManager should be injectable to any component, and not rely on events from UI. Those components should listen to BT permissions and BT initialization by themselves and act accordingly. Right now we have to init main notification components in a weird place, just because HubNative initialization is dependant on UI flow.

column1: ANDROID | One SSoT for translation files for both mobile platforms
column34:
h2. Context

Currently each Lens client is maintaining his own set of localisation files.

- [Android localisation {{strings.xml}}|https://github.azc.ext.hp.com/Poly-Lens-Apps/Lens-App-Android/blob/develop/app/src/main/res/values/strings.xml]
- [iOS {{Localizable.strings}}|https://github.azc.ext.hp.com/Poly-Lens-Apps/Lens-App-iOS/blob/develop/Monocle-iOS/Base.lproj/Localizable.strings]
- [Lens Desktop|https://github.azc.ext.hp.com/Poly-Lens-Apps/LensDesktop/tree/develop/src/renderer/assets/i18n]

As a {*}developer{*}, I need a *single file as post translation output* that can be used by the iOS and Android teams (for pages in the Lens app that are to be translated into supported languages). Having a single file provides a single common source, avoiding additional cost and time wastage.
h2. Acceptance Criteria

The dev team need to clearly understand the pre-translation file format that will be required from the dev team to enable successful translation
The post-translation file format, must be easily and efficiently ingestible into the app
There cannot be any errors (e.g. illegal characters) contained in the post translation file
The translation process must allow for incremental changes to take place, i.e. translating only what is needed rather than translating everything, when the UI is updated.
h1. Original description
h2. See epic for details

This is the Implementation story after the SPIKE (LENS-17074) completion.

column1: iOS | CANCELLED | DRAFT | Notify user about the micBoom position (DRAFT)
column34:
h3. Who/What/Why

As the end user of the poly headsets with boom, I'd like to be informed through the Lens app, if my headset's boom position is incorrect while I am on call and it's affecting my audio experience.
h3. Use case/Competitive feature parity 

Jabra app informs the user that the boom is not correctly positioned, so affecting the audio experience. 
Poly Lens mobile informs the correct position for earbuds for the fit test experience (VF60 and VF20).  However, headsets with a boom are not supported for the same user experience as of today.
h3. Acceptance criteria

Notify the end user if the boom position is not proper and there's an active audio channel detected (Tx and Rx) 
h3. Proposed solution: through the notification.

Based on the discussion with [~atrivedi], Boom sensors are supported on the Voyager Focus and Focus 2. (and potentially with Falcon headsets in the future as well)
{code:java}
<feature name="Mic Boom Position" id="0x021C">
<item name="Orientation" type="BYTE">
           <comment>
                 Current position of the boom.
            </comment>
            <definedValues name="MicBoomPositionSettingOrientation">
                     <definedValue name="mic_middle" value="0x00"/>
                     <definedValue name="mic_left"   value="0x01"/>
                     <definedValue name="mic_right"  value="0x02"/>
             </definedValues>
</item>
{code}
h3. Figma: TBD (!)

PLACEHOLDER - Not available yet
h3. NOTES
 - It needs more discovery on how this would work with firmware/UI Design/Lens Mobile.
 - For Voyager Focus, based on the firmware code shared above, the following is how it works:
 -- If the boom is "Up" then it will respond with 'middle', which means the boom position is close to the ear and not suitable for active speaking.
 -- Otherwise, it will respond with left or right depending on how the user is wearing it.

column1: iOS | CANCELLED | Create PoC for Lens Interactive Documentation JSON Data Format (DRAFT)
column34:
h2. (DRAFT)

{*}WHO{*}: Development Team
{*}WHAT{*}: Develop a PoC Android/iOS App for Lens Interactive Documentation JSON Data Format and compare Lottie Integration vs Android/iOS Native animations (sequence as described in the document, not the current implementation)
{*}WHY{*}: Validate JSON Schema and Identify Unanticipated Issues
h3. DESCRIPTION

Create of a Proof of Concept (PoC) Android/iOS app that adheres to the Lens Interactive Documentation JSON Data Format. The PoC will focus on integrating Lottie animations into the JSON schema to provide interactive elements and compare with the Android/iOS native animations. The primary purpose of this PoC is to thoroughly assess and validate the defined JSON schema, and identify any non-anticipated issues that may arise during implementation.
 
h3. BACKGROUND

[Lens Interactive Documentation Developer Guide|https://confluence.polycom.com:8443/pages/viewpage.action?spaceKey=HWS&title=Lens+Interactive+Documentation+Developer+Guide]

The Lens Interactive Documentation Data Format has been designed to enhance the user experience for Quick Guides and Onboarding Guides by providing a standardized and extensible format. To ensure the seamless integration of Lottie animations within the JSON schema.
 
h3. GOALS

1. *JSON Schema Validation:* The PoC app will validate the feasibility and effectiveness of the JSON schema, especially when integrated with Lottie animations
2. *Unanticipated Issues Detection:* Through the PoC, the Development Team aims to uncover any unexpected challenges or issues that may arise during the implementation of the defined data format and Lottie integration.
h3. BENEFITS
 - Early detection of challenges related to JSON schema and Lottie integration.
 - Identification of unanticipated technical or design obstacles.
 - Proactive problem-solving and refinement of the data format before full implementation.
 - Improved accuracy in estimating development efforts and timelines.

h3. OUTCOME

The PoC Android/iOS app, with integrated Lottie animations and Native animations based on the Lens Interactive Documentation JSON Data Format, will provide valuable insights into the viability and robustness of the schema.
h3. IN SCOPE / EFFORT BREAKDOWN

column1: ANDROID | CANCELLED | Create PoC for Lens Interactive Documentation Data Format (DRAFT)
column34:
h2. (DRAFT)

{*}WHO{*}: Development Team
{*}WHAT{*}: Develop a PoC Android/iOS App for Lens Interactive Documentation JSON Data Format and compare Lottie Integration vs Android/iOS Native animations (sequence as described in the document, not the current implementation)
{*}WHY{*}: Validate JSON Schema and Identify Unanticipated Issues
h3. DESCRIPTION

Create of a Proof of Concept (PoC) Android/iOS app that adheres to the Lens Interactive Documentation JSON Data Format. The PoC will focus on integrating Lottie animations into the JSON schema to provide interactive elements and compare with the Android/iOS native animations. The primary purpose of this PoC is to thoroughly assess and validate the defined JSON schema, and identify any non-anticipated issues that may arise during implementation.
 
h3. BACKGROUND

[Lens Interactive Documentation Developer Guide|https://confluence.polycom.com:8443/pages/viewpage.action?spaceKey=HWS&title=Lens+Interactive+Documentation+Developer+Guide]

The Lens Interactive Documentation Data Format has been designed to enhance the user experience for Quick Guides and Onboarding Guides by providing a standardized and extensible format. To ensure the seamless integration of Lottie animations within the JSON schema.
 
h3. GOALS

1. *JSON Schema Validation:* The PoC app will validate the feasibility and effectiveness of the JSON schema, especially when integrated with Lottie animations
2. *Unanticipated Issues Detection:* Through the PoC, the Development Team aims to uncover any unexpected challenges or issues that may arise during the implementation of the defined data format and Lottie integration.
h3. BENEFITS
 - Early detection of challenges related to JSON schema and Lottie integration.
 - Identification of unanticipated technical or design obstacles.
 - Proactive problem-solving and refinement of the data format before full implementation.
 - Improved accuracy in estimating development efforts and timelines.

h3. OUTCOME

The PoC Android/iOS app, with integrated Lottie animations and Native animations based on the Lens Interactive Documentation JSON Data Format, will provide valuable insights into the viability and robustness of the schema.
h3. IN SCOPE / EFFORT BREAKDOWN
h4. PREREQUISITES / DEPENDENCIES
 - Implement Lottie files {{UX TEAM}}
 - Provide final JSON schema with Lottie animations {{Cloud Team}}
 - Provide final JSON schema with Native animations {{Cloud Team}}

h4. IMPLEMENTATIONS

In separated apps with local schema:
 - Implement Lottie approach {{DEV}}
 - Implement Android/iOS Native implementation of animations {{DEV}}

h4. OUT OF SCOPE
 - Networking performances if JSON and Lotties are hosted, how fast the Lottie files will download?
 - Not describing the positioning of elements in the screen {{TO CONFIRM}}

h3. QUESTIONS
 - Where do we host the JSON and Lottie files? {{TO CONFIRM}}
 - Should we implement hardcoded placement of elements on the screen inside the JSON? {{TO CONFIRM}}

column1: iOS 💡 Feature Request ❄️ Show Bluetooth connections headsets have and allow user to clear as needed
column34:
*Who/What/Why:* 
As an end-user, I would like the feature to clear the Bluetooth device from all previous connections. So not only disconnect the device from other phones, but also make the Bluetooth device let go of all previous connections. 

 

*Acceptance criteria:* TBW by Engineering team after UX is defined

 

*Figma Spec:* [~kem.gallione@hp.com] 

 

*Notes from PO/PM:*

1. *Current solution:* 
The purpose of the device level setting "⁠[Clear Trusted Devices|https://rndwiki.inc.hpicorp.net/confluence/display/HBU/Reset]" is to allow the user to delete the list of connected devices stored on the headset.
For example, the user may be sharing the device with others. Or no longer wants the headset to appear in the list of paired Bluetooth devices, on other personal devices such as PC/smartphone/TV/car, etc. 

3. Useful feature based on the user feedback below in the description: VERY handy feature to ensure that you were the only one connected to the device.

4. Related tickets: LENS-13131, LENS-17395, LENS-11068

5. *Ask for improved user experience:* To offer a user experience for selectively disconnecting the poly headset/earbuds from other devices.

Competitive parity: The Bose app offers this. Please see the attached screenshot.

________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________

*Original ticket description:* (from Ravi)

As a user, I want to see all iOS/Android devices my headphones are paired with and be able to disable and remove any I no longer need paired.

I see a similar screen in the Bose app. (attached)

column1: ANDROID | OPSREY | Align Settings Dependencies Between Clients (Osprey)
column34:
In this ticket: LENS-11556 there was work done that aligned settings within the Cloud based off of Desktop for Osprey devices. This is a one-off change that may occur from time to time until there is work done in the Global Settings Feature.

The purpose of this ticket is to align Android mobile settings for Osprey the same way they're displayed in Desktop. In the Cloud there were certain settings that weren't available yet (such as *ANC Mode* and {*}Transparency Mode{*}) that we couldn't replicate yet but these seem to be available in the Android app.

 

*Acceptance Criteria:*
1. Setting Categories all match up and are in the correct order

2. Settings are in the correct categories

3. Setting dependencies are represented correctly

 

Here is a breakdown of the Desktop Categories and Setting behaviors for Osprey:
|*Category*|*Setting Name*|*Dependency*|*Action?*|
|(+) General|Mute Reminder Mode|*Toggle Dependencies*| |
|(+) General|Mute Reminder Timing|Dependent on *Mute Reminder Mode*|Hidden unless Mute Reminder Mode is set to *Time-Based*|
|(+) General|Second Incoming Call| | |
|(+) General|Mute On/Off Alerts| | |
|(+) General|Notification Tones| | |
|(+) General|Equalizer| | |
|(+) Voice Prompts & Tones|Manage All|*Toggle Dependencies*|Manages all settings in this category|
|(+) Voice Prompts & Tones|Calls|*Toggle Dependencies,* also dependent on *Manage All*|Visible but disabled if *Manage All* is toggled off|
|(+)Voice Prompts & Tones|Incoming Call|Dependent on *Manage All* as well as *Calls*|SEE Chart Below *|
|(+)Voice Prompts & Tones|Answering Call|Dependent on *Manage All* as well as *Calls*|SEE Chart Below *|
|(+)Voice Prompts & Tones|Answer/Ignore|Dependent on *Manage All* as well as *Calls*|SEE Chart Below *|
|(+)Voice Prompts & Tones|Active Audio Tone|Dependent on *Manage All* as well as *Calls*|SEE Chart Below *|
|(+) Voice Prompts & Tones|Connection|Dependent on *Manage All*|Visible but disabled if *Manage All* is toggled off|
|(+) Voice Prompts & Tones|Hold Reminder|Dependent on *Manage All*|Visible but disabled if *Manage All* is toggled off|
|(+) Ringtones & Volume|Sidetone| | |
|(+) Ringtones & Volume|Volume Level Tones| | |
|(+) Ringtones & Volume|Ringtone| | |
|(+) Ringtones & Volume|Volume Min/Max Alerts| | |
|(+) Sensors & Presence|Wearing Sensor|*Toggle Dependencies*| |
|(+) Sensors & Presence|Active Call Audio|Dependent on *Wearing Sensor*|Visible but disabled if *Wearing Sensor* is toggled to off|
|(+) Sensors & Presence|Auto-Answer|Dependent on *Wearing Sensor*|Visible but disabled if *Wearing Sensor* is toggled to off|
|(+) Sensors & Presence|Auto-Pause Music|Dependent on *Wearing Sensor*|Visible but disabled if *Wearing Sensor* is toggled to off|
|(+) Advanced|Anti Startle| | |

h1. Guidelines to create a JIRA ticket
h2. What are we doing, as a ticket title?

Identify the object and what is the action? 
{color:#505f79}_e.g. Add support for Emu device to Lens mobile app_{color}
h2. Explain the Who, What and Why

Structure the requirement into a single sentence, 
{color:#505f79}_e.g. as a customer, I want to this because of that._{color}

As a {*}type of user{*}, I want *to perform some action* so I {*}reach some goal{*}.
{color:#505f79}_e.g. As a website visitor, I want the option to reset my password so that I can regain access to my account if I forget it._{color}
h2. What is the Acceptance Criteria

An itemised list that a developer or QA can use to test that the requirements have been met.
h3. Rule-oriented AC

{color:#505f79}e.g. 
- User sees a "Forgot Password" link on the login page.
- User receives a password reset email within 5 minutes of requesting it.
- The reset link expires and becomes invalid after 24 hours.
- User can successfully reset the password using the provided link.{color}
h3. Scenario-oriented AC

Given that [some context], when [some action is carried out], then [a set of observable outcomes should occur].
h2. What are the Dependencies?

A list of blockers or dependencies that the development and QA teams need to be aware of.
h2. What are the timelines or Schedules?

List the delivery dates for key milestones in the main body. The *epic* issue should contain the overall date the *epic* is scheduled to be closed.
h2. What is Out-of-Scope?

List items that are out-of-scope for this ticket to prevent over-delivery. Out-of-scope items may be requirements in the future, as part of new requirements.
h2. Is there any Additional Information?

Are there any example or references that the development teams should be aware of or take into consideration.

Notes from PO/PM:

1. Current solution:
The purpose of the device level setting "⁠Clear Trusted Devices" is to allow the user to delete the list of connected devices stored on the headset.
For example, the user may be sharing the device with others. Or no longer wants the headset to appear in the list of paired Bluetooth devices, on other personal devices such as PC/smartphone/TV/car, etc. 

3. Useful feature based on the user feedback below in the description: VERY handy feature to ensure that you were the only one connected to the device.

4. Related tickets: LENS-13131, LENS-17395, LENS-14251.

5. Ask for improved user experience: To offer a user experience for selectively disconnecting the poly headset/earbuds from other devices.

Competitive parity: The Bose app offers this. Please see the attached screenshot.

________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________

Original ticket description:

Poly Lens Localization Support

Reported By
jonas.vanderkaaij@poly.com

Details
With the old Plantronics HUB for mobile, you were able ro actually clear the Bluetooth device from all previous connections. So not only disconnect the device from one particular phone on the phone level, but also to make the Bluetooth device to let go of all previous connections. Previously the PLT/Poly headsets could remember up to 8 different phones, tablets and USB dongles, and this was a VERY handy feature to ensure that you were the only one connected to the device, for example when several USB dongles had been in use... I have not been able to find this feature on the Poly Lens for mobile (nor the desktop version for that matter) and really wish for it to be added as feature!! kind regards Jonas vd kaaij Olsson (headset alpha tester since 2015)

column1: ANDROID | DISCOVERY | Show Bluetooth devices connected to other lens client on user login (SPIKE)
column34:
As an end-user of the Lens client, I would like to see all my Bluetooth devices supported in the mobile app, if those are already connected to the other Lens client (Lens Desktop) when I log in using my Lens account on the Lens Mobile app.

This enables the soft onboarding of devices that are already connected to the other Lens clients, for the same user, if logged in with the same account.
The supported Bluetooth devices detected on the other Lens client can then show up as a "Disconnected" state until the end-user pairs it via Bluetooth.

 

Assumptions:

The user has a Lens account and is logged into other Lens clients, for example, Lens Desktop or other Lens Mobile apps, and has a supported Bluetooth device/devices, either currently connected/ or ever connected in the past, but showing disconnected status currently. 

 

Figma:

There's no Figma change needed. When the user logs in to the Lens account, if the devices connected to the Lens Desktop are reported to the Lens cloud, the API should fetch that list, and the Lens mobile app UI will then show the supported devices in the app with the disconnected status.  Please note that the Lens Mobile app only supports a subset of the devices that the Lens Desktop app supports. 
Supported Devices list on Lens Mobile: https://docs.poly.com/bundle/poly-lens-mobile/page/supported-devices.html 

 

Scope of investigation:

1. Test the existing API, to get the devices connected to the user's account. The ID should change for the Lens Mobile.

https://api.silica-prod01.io.lens.poly.com/graphql?&gqlVars=&gqlName=device 
 

The response should return the devices attached.

For example:

{
  "data": {
    "device": {
      "name": "Poly Lens Desktop",
      "user": {
        "user_id": "google-oauth2|114409457491754259395"
      },
      "tenantId": "323bba8b-7fc8-4fb0-9775-536e4e23d8b8",
      "connected": true,
      "connections": [
        {
          "name": "BT700",
          "id": "af8c8c3c7d89470f993cafe4554cfcc6",
          "serialNumber": "FB8GAW"
        },
        {
          "name": "Voyager 5200 Series",
          "id": "f223a6818f2f6267c583",
          "serialNumber": "2CBYMF"
        },
        {
          "name": "Blackwire 5220",
          "id": "22fe0a1c7d124a968fbb3ae8e2d78b46",
          "serialNumber": "25DUN4"
        }
      ]
    }
  }
}
column830:
With [~mateusz.wojtowicz@hp.com] we got several questions on the way when analyzing the ticket:


 # As for now, we have an option for the user to remove a device from the list locally in the app, so it no longer appears on the home screen. As the description suggest, we would present the device if it was ever connected to the account. Is it planned to keep an option of removing the device, or would we stick to the growing list of all the previously connected devices?
 # What is the "id" that we receive from the cloud, is it genes global guid? If so, what in case of earbuds with many genes guid, would we receive the array of genes for all the components? on the Android client side we store devices in database with their bluetooth address (which stands as the id that is the same for all its components) which we don't receive from the cloud. Can we reconsider to have unique global id for the whole device, (such as bluetooth address as we use it on our client side), which is consistent for all the components and can be used to differentiate the devices on our client side (as we do currently with the bluetooth address)?
 # What is meant by "soft onboarding"? Lack of the full onboarding or something else?
 # Should we fetch the device list on every login action? Would there be an event that inform us when the list of devices change on any other client/cloud (so we don't need to worry about not updating frequent enough)?
 # Would there be provided the FMD data from the cloud so we can enable this Find My Device feature for all of the cloud fetched devices?
 # As for FMD, follow up question, would there be a data about the battery provided too so we can correctly present all the device data in our app, as if it was previously connected?
 # As for previous two, follow up question, would there be data provided for the Support screen where we include device info about product id, (serial number is already included in the json example), software version and last connected date?
 # What kind of behaviour is expected before the user is logged in the first time, as there is no previous connection or cache from the cloud? 
 # Follow up question to the previous one, what after the user log out from the account? should we present data from the cache or treat it as the case without previous login action?

column1: iOS | CANCELED | Align setting jsons with Lens desktop in iOS app
column34:
Currently there is a diffrence between setting.json for desktop and mobile setting.json.

Because there is a initiative for settings to be pulled from server, we need to align json format with Lens desktop. Also it would be easier for self-service or dev implementation of settings for particular device because it will be only copy/paste from desktop git and now we need to convert json to mobile format.

Examples of jsons are in attachment. Mobile codebase should be updated to use desktop json format.

Location of all dekstop jsons:
https://onecode.polycom-labs.com/poly-glass/lens-apps/lensdesktop/-/tree/develop/native/DeviceSettingFiles 

Note that mobile platform needs only subset of jsons. Files are named by pids and supported pids on mobile platforms could be found here: https://confluence.polycom.com:8443/display/PLYGS/Lens+Mobile+MVP+for+FA
